synopsis: Two young British soldiers during the First World War, are given an impossible mission: deliver a message, deep in enemy territory, that will stop their own men, and Blake’s own brother, from walking straight into a deadly trap.

In 1980s Texas, a closeted young man returns home from New York for Christmas carrying a heavy secret. As he reconnects with his conservative family, he must navigate love, loss, and the fear of being truly seen—before it’s too late.

36 is the number of shots on an analogue roll of film. It’s also the number of shots in this film. Yet it’s not a strict film, but the playful quest of a young photographer for the photos that disappeared on her computer: a whole year’s worth, including one of a challenging encounter.

With cameras in hand, directors Bill and Turner Ross return to their hometown of Sidney, Ohio—zip code 45365—for nine months. In this small town, the stories of a father and son, cops and criminals, officials and their electorate coalesce into a mosaic of faces, places, and events.

title: 8½
synopsis: Struggling to find inspiration for his next project, acclaimed director Guido Anselmi wrestles with his dreams, memories and desires in his search for creativity. Blending visions with reality, his artistic crisis leads him down many a strange and twisted paths, as his life unravels around him.

Chow Mo-Wan was a writer. He thought he wrote about the future but it really was the past. In his novel, a mysterious train left for 2046 every once in a while. Everyone who went there had the same intention: to recapture their lost memories. It was said that in 2046, nothing ever changed.

title: 100 Yards
synopsis: In volatile 1920s Tianjin, a covert martial arts circle arose to deter crime and maintain peace between rival fighting arts schools. When a renowned master dies and names his apprentice—rather than his son—as his successor, a fierce power struggle will bring the entire city to the brink of chaos.
Images of cherry blossoms, luminous fields bursting with color, and miscellaneous domestic ephemera are photographed from the screen of an obsolete televisual device.

title: 10/65: Self-Mutilation
synopsis: The film is developed from a Gunter Brus’ action. It exposes each gesture so that one gets a tense, iconoclastic revelation of a man covered in white plaster, surrounded by razor blades and a range of instruments looking as if they have been taken from an operating theater.

title: 12 Years a Slave
synopsis: In the pre-Civil War U.S., Solomon Northup, a free Black man from New York, is abducted and sold into slavery. Solomon struggles to stay alive and retain his dignity, facing cruelty and unexpected instances of kindness. But his chance meeting with a Canadian abolitionist will forever alter his life.

title: BPM (Beats Per Minute)
synopsis: In the early 90s, as the AIDS epidemic tears through their community, the members of ACT UP Paris are fighting for survival. While battling against governmental apathy, pharmaceutical greed, and public ignorance, the activists party all night, debate politics—and occasionally fall in love.

title: 12/66: Cosinus Alpha
synopsis: Kren’s last film to include any direct reference to Otto Muehl is 12/66: Cosinus Alpha, a 10-minute film that completes a cycle of elaborate sensual montages and materials.

title: 10c/65: Brus Wishes You a Merry Christmas
synopsis: A kind of home movie made in Brus’ apartment. Brus’ Christmas wishes can be seen on a poster which he painted and which he holds for a short time in front of the camera.

title: 12 Hours
synopsis: During the height of the pandemic, Shirin returns to her family home to care for her dying, estranged father. Convinced he has hours to live, she summons her siblings—but her paranoia has already sealed him away. As buried conflicts resurface, an unexpected bond offers a fragile chance at healing.

title: 13 Assassins
synopsis: Shimada leads a team of assassins in 19th-century Japan to kill the ruthless Lord Matsudaira, who is wreaking havoc against his own people. Hired secretly by a government official hoping to end this reign of terror, Shimada recruits the best samurai to set a trap for the lord’s faithful bodyguards.

title: 12th Fail
synopsis: The real-life story of IPS Officer Manoj Kumar Sharma and IRS Officer Shraddha Joshi.

title: 13 Tzameti
synopsis: 22 year-old Sébastien leads an impoverished life struggling to support his family. While repairing a neighbor’s roof, he overhears a conversation about a forthcoming package which promised to make the household rich. He intercepts the mysterious package—which comes with a cryptic set of clues.

title: 2 or 3 Things I Know About Her
synopsis: Juliette Janson, a housewife from the Paris suburbs, lives in a high-rise apartment with her husband and two children. As consumer culture in Paris booms, Juliette decides to start a part-time job as a sex worker to afford some of life’s little luxuries and escape suburbia.

title: 2 Days in New York
synopsis: Talk-radio host Mingus and his French photographer girlfriend, Marion, live cozily in a New York apartment with their cat and two young children from previous relationships. When Marion’s father, sister, and her sister’s outrageous boyfriend descend upon them for an overseas visit, mayhem ensues.

title: 20,000 Species of Bees
synopsis: An eight-year-old child struggles with the fact that people keep addressing her in confusing ways. During a summer in the Basque Country among the beehives, she explores her identity alongside the women of her family, who at the same time reflect on their own lives and desires.

title: Spring Blossom
synopsis: Suzanne is sixteen. She is bored with people her own age. Every day on her way to high school, she passes a theater. There, she meets a 35-year-old actor named Raphaël. Despite their age difference they find in each other an answer to their ennui and develop a strong connection.

title: 2022 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Live Action
director: Martin Strange-Hansen, Kristen Davila, Tadeusz Łysiak, Aneil Karia, Maria Brendle
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Live Action category at the 2022 Academy Awards : On My Mind, Please Hold, The Dress, The Long Goodbye, and Ala Kachuu: Take and Run.

title: 2022 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Animation
director: Daniel Ojari, Michael Please, Anton Dyakov, Joanna Quinn, Hugo Covarrubias, Alberto Mielgo
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Animation category at the 2022 Academy Awards : Robin Robin, Boxballet, Affairs of the Art, Bestia, and The Windshield Wiper.

title: 2023 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Animation
director: Lachlan Pendragon, Amanda Forbis, Wendy Tilby, João Gonzalez, Bruno Caetano, Charlie Mackesy, Matthew Freud, Sara Gunnarsdóttir, Pamela Ribon
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Animation category at the Academy Awards®: The Flying Sailor, Ice Merchants, My Year of Dicks, An Ostrich Told Me… and The Boy, the Mole, the Fox and the Horse.

title: 2023 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Live Action
director: Anders Walter, Rebecca Pruzan, Eirik Tveiten, Gaute Lid Larssen, Cyrus Neshvad, Tom Berkeley, Ross White
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Live Action category at the Academy Awards®: Ivalu, Night Ride, Le Pupille, The Red Suitcase and An Irish Goodbye.

title: 2024 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Live Action
director: Wes Anderson, Misan Harriman, Vincent René-Lortie, Lasse Lyskjær Noer, Nazrin Choudhury
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Live Action category at the Academy Awards®: The After, Invincible, Knight of Fortune, Red, White and Blue and The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar.

title: 2025 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Animation
director: Nina Gantz, Hossein Molayemi, Shirin Sohani, Nicolas Keppens, Loïc Espuche, Daisuke Nishio
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Animation category at the Academy Awards®: Wander to Wonder, In the Shadow of Cypress, Beautiful Men, Yuck! and Magic Candies.

title: 2025 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Live Action
director: Sam Cutler-Kreutz, David Cutler-Kreutz, Victoria Warmerdam, Adam J. Graves, Nebojša Slijepčević, Cindy Lee
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Live Action category at the Academy Awards®: A Lien, I’m Not a Robot, The Last Ranger, Anuja and The Man Who Could Not Remain Silent.

title: 2026 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Animation
director: Florence Miailhe, Chris Lavis, Maciek Szczerbowski, John Kelly, Jeremy Spears, Nathan Engelhardt, Konstantin Bronzit
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Animation category at the Academy Awards®: Butterfly, The Girl Who Cried Pearls, Retirement Plan, Forevergreen and The Three Sisters.

title: 2026 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Live Action
director: Alexandre Singh, Natalie Musteata, Lee Knight, Sam A. Davis, Julia Aks, Steve Greist, Meyer Levinson-Blount
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Live Action category at the Academy Awards®: Two People Exchanging Saliva, A Friend of Dorothy, The Singers, Jane Austen’s Period Drama and Butcher’s Stain.

title: 2024 Oscar® Nominated Short Films: Animation
director: Tal Kantor, Jerusha Hess, Jared Hess, Yegane Moghaddam, Stéphanie Clément, Dave Mullins
synopsis: Compilation of all five films nominated in the Short Films: Animation category at the Academy Awards®: Letter to a Pig, Ninety-Five Senses, Our Uniform, Pachyderme and War Is Over! Inspired by the Music of John & Yoko.

title: 20th Century Women
synopsis: In Santa Barbara, determined single mother Dorothea is in her 50s and raising her teenage son Jamie at a moment of cultural change. To help Jamie’s upbringing she enlists two younger women: the free-spirited artist Abbie lodging in Dorothea’s home, and Julie, a savvy, provocative teenage neighbor.

title: #21XOXO
synopsis: A young woman drifts through a parallel digital world—searching for love by navigating speed dating, hipster culture, and post-net detachment. The woman’s adventure becomes increasingly surreal with each fleeting encounter and virtual connection.

title: 24 Hour Party People
synopsis: A vibrant portrait of the Manchester music scene during the mid-to-late 1970s, through the eyes of TV presenter Tony Wilson, founder of Factory Records, as he introduces the world to such notable musical acts as Joy Division, New Order, and The Happy Mondays.

title: 28 Days Later
synopsis: A group of animal rights advocates breaks into a research laboratory, releasing a caged chimpanzee that is infected with the rage virus. When a bike courier wakes from a coma in an abandoned London hospital 28 days later, he embarks on a perilous journey to escape from the infected.

Alice still lives with her parents and tends to live in her dreams to escape her dreary everyday life. After a psychedelic party on a factory roof, she has a serious drunken bike accident. Will this give her the courage to become an adult?

title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple
synopsis: As Spike is inducted into Jimmy Crystal’s gang on the mainland, Dr. Kelson makes a discovery that could alter the world.

title: 28 Years Later
synopsis: A group of survivors of the rage virus lives on a small island. When one of the group leaves the island on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors.

title: 25 Ways to Quit Smoking
synopsis: Noted animator Bill Plympton helps you kick the habit by illustrating 25 ways to curb your nicotine cravings that are effective, simple, and often far more hazardous to your health than a 5-pack-a-day habit. Beware the sumo treatment!

title: 3 Faces
synopsis: Filmmaker Jafar Panahi and actor Behnaz Jafari travel to a tiny village after receiving a plea for help from a girl whose family has forbidden her from studying acting. Amusing encounters abound, but they soon discover that the local hospitality is rivaled by the desire to protect old traditions.

title: 3 Shots
synopsis: An evening get-together of a group of vampire friends takes an interesting turn when an unsuspecting human visitor arrives.

title: 4 Days in France
synopsis: On a whim, Pierre abandons his Parisian apartment—and his boyfriend—for the lush countryside of southern France. Letting himself be guided by the dating app Grindr, he wanders from landscape to landscape, man to man. Meanwhile, his jilted lover tries to discover his location through the same app.

title: 45 Years
synopsis: It’s one week until Kate and Geoff’s 45th anniversary, and a huge party is being planned. But then a letter arrives for Geoff. The body of Katya, his first love, has been discovered frozen and immaculately preserved in the glaciers of the Swiss Alps. Kate becomes obsessed with Geoff’s past.

title: 5x2
synopsis: Gilles and Marion, a young French couple, separate. Told in reverse, the story traces five key moments of their relationship: their divorce, a strained dinner with friends, the birth of their child, their wedding, and their first meeting.

title: 6/64: Mama and Papa: An Otto Mühl Happening
synopsis: With the making of his sixth film, Kren introduced subject-matter that was considered at that time to be highly revolutionary or even explosive. He began filming ‘actions’ and ‘happenings’ staged by Otto Muehl and Günter Brus, and by the Vienna Institute for Direct Art.

title: 7/64: Leda and the Swan
synopsis: Kren´s film 7/64 – Leda and the Swan is also based on a Muehl Materialaktion. The almost convulsive use of juxtaposition reappears here, but the captured gesture assumes a more erotic sensitivity, though the “action” itself was primarily a gradual destruction of the erotic.

title: 8 Women
synopsis: In an isolated mansion in 1950s France, a family is gathered for the holiday season. But the patriarch is found murdered, and the killer can only be one of the eight women closest to him. The telephone line has been cut, the car won’t start, and a heavy snowfall keeps the women confined together.

title: 9 Muses of Star Empire
synopsis: Nine young women are followed for a year as they train to be the next K-pop sensations. Chosen by the powerful Star Empire Agency to be part of a new girl group called 9 Muses, the women undergo intense scrutiny as they are molded into an image created by the CEO.

title: 9 Months 9 Days
synopsis: August 9 2006. Three Mexican fishermen are found near the Marshall Islands, more than 5,000 miles away from home, after 9 months and 9 days adrift. As their story spreads, iIt seems that the real danger is still to come.

title: 9½ Weeks
synopsis: Elizabeth, a divorced New York art gallery curator, finds herself drawn into an obsessive sexual relationship with John, a compelling Wall Street broker. At first, the relationship is stimulating and exciting, but as their sex games become increasingly perverse, she begins to fear for her sanity.

title: A Better Tomorrow
synopsis: Two friends, Ho (Ti Lung) and Mark (Chow Yun Fat), are triads in a counterfeiting operation who end up doing ‘one more job’ and what do you know, this one more job gets messier than they had hoped…

title: 9/64: O Christmas Tree
synopsis: Kren offers a more visually descriptive development of a Muehl action. The images have been chosen to follow a more dramatic sequence, probably because the action itself contained a wide range of images and materials.

title: A Bigger Splash
synopsis: A high-profile couple—famous rock star Marianne Lane and filmmaker Paul—are holidaying on the picturesque secluded Italian island of Pantelleria. But their tranquil retreat is interrupted by the arrival of an old friend and charismatic music producer, Harry, and his daughter, Penelope.

title: 9x10 novanta
director: Alice Rohrwacher, Pietro Marcello, Giovanni Piperno, Alina Marazzi, Marco Bonfanti, Claudio Giovannesi, Costanza Quatriglio, Roland Sejko, Paola Randi, Sara Fgaier
synopsis: Young Italian filmmakers create nine ten-minute shorts using unseen footage from the Istituto Luce’s archives. Each short reflects on Italy’s recent history—from wartime to peace, from ruins to reconstruction, and from Fascism’s lost promised futures to the present day.

title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
synopsis: An imaginative tale of two strangers and the unbelievable journey that connects them.

title: A Brighter Summer Day
synopsis: Taiwan, 1960s. After failing a test, innocent boy Xiao Si’r must attend night school. There, two rival gangs constantly clash. Xiao Si’r stays away from the gangs’ actions—until the day he meets Ming and falls in love with her. But Ming is the girlfriend of Honey, leader of one of the two gangs…

title: A Bronx Tale
synopsis: In 1960s Bronx, teenager Calogero is drawn to local mob boss Sonny, who becomes an unlikely mentor. Torn between his father’s values and the pull of street life, Calogero navigates loyalty, violence, and first love.

title: A Cambodian Spring
synopsis: Over the course of six years, three land-rights activists are caught up in the chaotic and often violent wave of change that is shaping modern-day Cambodia. The three speak out against the injustices taking place all around them in the name of progress, development, and democracy.

title: A Chiara
synopsis: The story of 15-year-old Chiara whose close-knit family falls apart after her father abandons them in Calabria. Chiara starts to investigate to understand why her father disappeared and as she gets closer to the truth, she is forced to decide what kind of future she wants for herself.

title: A Christmas Tale
synopsis: Junon is the matriarch of the troubled Vuillard family, who come together at Christmas after she learns she needs a bone marrow transplant from a blood relative. But this is no simple family reunion, as blinding grudges, unrequited loves, and brutal outbursts come to the surface.

title: A Complete Unknown
synopsis: A young Bob Dylan shakes up the folk music scene when he plugs in his electric guitar at the Newport Folk Festival in 1965.

title: A Couple
synopsis: A film about a long term relationship between a man and a woman. The man is Leo Tolstoy. The woman is his wife, Sophia. They were married for 36 years and had 13 children. Each kept a diary. Although they lived together, in the same house, they wrote letters to each other frequently.

title: A Cup of Turkish Coffee
synopsis: An old woman and her granddaughter sit around a table and go through old family photographs. The old woman tries to resist looking at these pictures, yet she cannot resist what the past evokes.

title: A Date for Mad Mary
synopsis: Mary returns to her small Irish hometown after a brief stint in prison. Her best friend, Charlene, is about to get married, and Mary is the maid of honor. However, when Charlene refuses Mary a plus one, believing she’ll struggle to find a date, Mary becomes determined to prove her wrong.

title: A Different Kind of Mourn
synopsis: Gün returns to her hometown following the death of her beloved grandmother. Although overwhelmed by the intricacies of mourning ceremonies, she manages to navigate the obstacles one by one. However, one significant obligation remains insurmountable.

title: A Different Man
synopsis: An aspiring actor undergoes a radical medical procedure to drastically transform his appearance. However, his new dream face quickly turns into a nightmare, as he loses out on the role he was born to play and becomes obsessed with reclaiming what was lost.

title: A Dirty Story
synopsis: A group of friends listen as one man tells them a story about a time when, in a small cafe, he discovered a peephole into the ladies’ bathroom and became addicted to looking through it at female genitals. They ask him questions and come to conclusions about sex.

title: A Dog Called Money
synopsis: Award-winning war photographer and filmmaker Seamus Murphy explores the creative inspiration behind PJ Harvey’s album The Hope Six Demolition Project, filming her in a London recording studio and charting their travels together in Afghanistan, Kosovo and the U.S.

title: A Dream Longer Than the Night
synopsis: Magically aged into adulthood, Princess Camélia traverses a phantasmagoric landscape of enchanted beings, goblins, and ghouls. On her journey through this surreal world, she confronts a series of frightful encounters and the perils of patriarchy awaiting her in womanhood.

title: A E I O U: A Quick Alphabet of Love
synopsis: Anna, an out-of-work actress in Berlin, is mugged by a young man. But later that day, she meets him again… as his speech teacher. Their lessons grow into an unlikely friendship, then a pact. Cheered on by each other’s affection, without a penny to their name, they leave town and head for the sea.

title: A Family Film
synopsis: Set in 1970s Istanbul, A Family Film captures the everyday life of a family through Super 8mm home movies, with the mother’s narration guiding viewers through their shared memories and moments.

title: A Field in England
synopsis: England, the 1640s Civil War. A group of men flee from a raging battle but they are captured and forced to take part in a hunt for treasure supposedly buried somewhere in a field. But before they start digging, they gobble up some strange-looking mushrooms and then everything goes pear-shaped.

title: A Fantastic Woman
synopsis: Marina, a trans nightclub singer, comes under suspicion when her much older cis lover Orlando suddenly dies. After being harassed and threatened by her late boyfriend’s vengeful relatives and the police, she must defend her rights as both Orlando’s partner and as a human being.

title: A Fish Called Wanda
synopsis: An ambitious and sexy con artist uses every tool at her disposal to obtain a fortune in jewels stolen by her mobster lover. To help in her plan, she woo’s first her lover’s deadly henchman and then his attorney, eventually falling in love with one of them.

title: A Funny Man
synopsis: Comedian Dirch Passer has found himself in perennial loneliness after a fast-tracked rise to fame. He struggles between his own desire to gain critical respect and servicing the audience’s needs. His talent is both a blessing and a curse…

title: A Girl Walks Home Alone at Night
synopsis: In the shadows of Bad City–a lawless hellhole, inhabited by underworld figures, drug users, and pimps–a lonesome vampire wanders around. Dressed in a hijab and a striped t-shirt, she follows local villagers. One night she meets Arash, an Iranian James Dean lookalike. There is an instant attraction.

title: A Guide to Recognizing Your Saints
synopsis: Set in 1980s Astoria, Queens, Dito, called home after 15 years because his father is ill, encounters old friends—the ones he lost, the ones he left behind the ones he can’t help but remember.

title: A Glitch in the Matrix
synopsis: What if we are living in a simulation and the world as we know it is not real? Using a famous speech from Philip K. Dick, the filmmaker dives down the rabbit hole of science, philosophy, and conspiracy theory,  interviewing digital avatars and unpacking cultural touchstones like The Matrix.

title: A Happy Event
synopsis: Celebrated French director Rémi Bezançon’s incisive, yet tender and humorous, third feature follows a young couple whose carefree lifestyle is interrupted by the birth of their first child, a happy event that also turns their world completely upside-down.

title: A Hero
synopsis: Rahim is in prison because of a debt he was unable to repay. During a two-day leave, he tries to convince his creditor to withdraw his complaint against the payment of part of the sum. But things don’t go as planned.

title: A Hidden Life
synopsis: Based on real events, this is the story of an unsung hero, Franz Jägerstätter, who refused to fight for the Nazis in WWII. When the Austrian peasant farmer is faced with the threat of execution, it is his unwavering faith and his love for his wife Fani and children that keeps his spirit alive.

title: A Hero of Our Time
synopsis: Mert, an immature man in his thirties, lives in Istanbul but must travel to a small northeastern town for his father’s funeral. Over three days, as he avoids problems and confronts tragicomic events, he’s forced to face the growing cracks in his life and undergo a sudden, intense transformation.

title: A House Made of Splinters
synopsis: Lysychansk, eastern Ukraine. The staff at an interim care center do all they can to meet the housing and emotional needs of neglected children. Removed from broken homes, the young wards form strong friendships as they await court custody decisions about their futures.

title: A Journey in Spring
synopsis: In a marriage spanning years, Khim-Hok and Siu-Tuan are inseparable yet conflicted. When Siu-Tuan suddenly passes away, Khim-Hok clings desperately to her, but he must still traverse all past memories in the moist embrace of spring, bidding a heartfelt farewell.

title: A Letter to Uncle Boonmee
synopsis: Filming in Nabua, site of a bloody 1965 battle between communist farmers and the totalitarian government, Apichatpong employs a roving, floating camera and incantatory, omniscient narration to simultaneously evoke the dangerous cycles of violence and repression.

title: Kantara
synopsis: Shiva, a tribal vagabond lives with his mother in hamlet, stays away from the traditional Daivaradhane and Bhoota Kola legacy due to an unforgettable childhood incident. When Forest officer Murali enters the scene, it gives a fresh dimension to the man-vs-nature fight.

title: A Long Journey Into the Truth of Cinema: Necip Sarıcı
synopsis: Necip Sarıcı began his cinema journey at a young age as a projectionist and worked as a sound engineer on hundreds of films at Lale Film Studio throughout his career. He also produced many films, including The Well (1968) and Oh Beautiful Istanbul (1982).

title: A Long Winter
synopsis: Set high in the mountains, fall comes to an end and a family prepares for the long winter ahead.

title: A Magnificent Haunting
synopsis: Pietro is a young, gay pastry chef. He decides to leave Sicily and move to Rome in order to pursue his dream of becoming an actor. In Rome, he has to built his own support system in order to cope with setbacks and success.

title: A Man Called Ove
synopsis: Ove is your typical angry old man next door. Enter a lively young family who accidentally flattens Ove’s mailbox while moving in. Yet from this inauspicious beginning an unlikely friendship forms, exemplifying a tale of unreliable first impressions and the reminder that life is sweeter when shared.

title: A Most Violent Year
synopsis: New York, 1981. As the city experiences a spike in criminal activity, Abel Morales—a respectable heating business owner—becomes the target of thieves. Abel takes matters into his own hands to track down those responsible. In doing so, he attracts the attention of the assistant district attorney.

title: A Most Wanted Man
synopsis: A mysterious, near-dead half-Chechen, half-Russian man arrives in Hamburg’s Islamic community desperate for help. Nothing about him adds up; is he a victim, or an extremist intent on destruction? The brilliant, roguish chief of a German spy unit fights to put the pieces together as the clock ticks.

title: A Mother's Sonata
synopsis: It’s just another weird and ordinary day for Nesrin and her mother. As the evening comes, they face the same issues they face everyday again but the next day their ordinary flow of lies is about to be broken.

title: A Mysterious World
synopsis: Boris is perplexed as Ana seeks time apart, leaving their future uncertain. He navigates an erratic urban journey, from a transient hotel to a temperamental car, chance encounters, and efforts to reconcile with Ana amid the ambiguity of their separation.

title: A Night of Knowing Nothing
synopsis: An Indian film student writes letters to her boyfriend from whom she was forcibly separated. She reflects on her daily life, disillusionments, and inner thoughts—her voice blending with images of unrest in university campuses, press clippings, and archival footage from Narendra Modi’s rise to power.

title: A Perfect Day
synopsis: A jealous and violent bodyguard can’t accept his ex-wife’s abandonment.

title: A Personal Journey with Martin Scorsese Through American Movies
synopsis: Martin Scorsese explores the history of American cinema by focusing on the work of a number of highly influential filmmakers. In addition to examining pioneering productions by D.W. Griffith and F.W. Murnau, he looks at a variety of key directors, while featuring interviews with cinema luminaries.

title: A Place Without Fear
synopsis: In a haunting, dream-like world, a young girl navigates a labyrinth of physical and emotional challenges within the decaying walls of an abandoned house. There, she must choose between succumbing to her fears or transforming into a formidable force.

title: A Poet
synopsis: Oscar Restrepo’s obsession with poetry brought him no glory. Aging and erratic, he has succumbed to the cliché of the poet in the shadows. Meeting Yurlady, a humble teenager, brings some light to his days, but dragging her into the world of poets may not be the way.

title: Full Time
synopsis: Julie can’t catch a break. For a single mother raising two children in the suburbs of Paris but working in the city, the train is a lifeline, and it’s suddenly severed during a strike. Without the train, Julie can’t get to her job at the hotel—or to the interview for a dream job she has lined up.

title: A Private War
synopsis: War correspondent Marie Colvin is a woman who is as comfortable downing martinis with high society’s elite as she is staring down warlords and fleeing from gunfire. Driven by a desire to give voice to the voiceless, Colvin charges into danger, testing the limits between bravery and bravado.

title: A Proletarian Winter's Tale
synopsis: Three Georgians have to clean a castle where an arms manufacturer’s art collection is on exhibit. They aren’t welcome at the opening party and are banished to the attic, but downstairs the splendid buffet attracts them. Why not just ignore the unfair prohibition and cross the line of class society?

title: A Quiet Passion
synopsis: Emily Dickinson, now recognized as a genius that penned some of the most important verses in American literature, was virtually unknown in her lifetime. From her days as a schoolgirl through to her later years as a recluse, the lack of respect for her work stokes ever more vociferous dissension.

title: A Real Pain
synopsis: Two estranged cousins reunite for a tour through Poland to honor their beloved grandmother. The adventure takes a turn when the pair’s old tensions resurface against the backdrop of their family history.

title: A Real Young Girl
synopsis: Alice, a sulky teenage girl, reluctantly returns to her parents’ house for yet another boring summer vacation. Preyed by sexual desire and caged fantasies, she fantasizes about Jim, her father’s young employee, and tries to seduce him by all means.

title: A Quiet Summer
synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Ada faces pressures from her family as she tries to have her first summer fling in Turkey. Things take a turn for the worst when her carefree teenage cousin Mira arrives from France.

title: A Room in Town
synopsis: In this musical melodrama set against the backdrop of a workers’ strike in Nantes, a young woman wishes to leave her brutish husband for an earthy steelworker, though he is involved with another. Unbeknownst to the girl, the object of her affection boards with her no-nonsense baroness mother.

title: A River Called Titas
synopsis: A fisherman, Kishore, marries Basanti when he visits a nearby village. After their wedding night, she is kidnapped on the river. When she is found, she has amnesia—although she does not remember her new husband’s name or what he looks like, she does remember the name of his village.

title: A Regular Woman
synopsis: Hatun Ayhrun Sürücü, a German woman of Kurdish-Turkish descent, was shot dead by her brothers at a Berlin bus stop on February 7, 2005. These are the events that led to her death, as she struggled for freedom and self-determined life in the face of her family’s opposition.

title: A Run for Money
synopsis: Selim is a shop owner in Istanbul. Selim is an affectionate family man who has devoted his life to his home and his work, and is known for his honesty and integrity. One day, Selim finds a bag full of dollar bills in a taxi. This brings a small crime and a big tragedy to him and his family.

title: A Scene at the Sea
synopsis: Shigeru, a young deaf garbage collector, finds a broken surfboard in a trash can. Soon, he becomes obsessed with surfing. Despite multiple setbacks, Shigeru may finally have a chance to become one with the sea and the surfing community with the support of his longtime girlfriend, Takako.

title: A Season in Hakkari
synopsis: An urban Turkish teacher is transferred for political reasons to a remote village in the mountains near the Iranian border of Eastern Anatolia. He is welcomed with distrust, but during that harsh year the mutual cultural misunderstandings fade away.

title: A Screaming Man
synopsis: Adam Ousmane is a pool attendant at a local resort. When the new managers decide to downsize, Adam loses his job to his own son, Abdel. Shattered by the turn of events, Adam is pressured into contributing to the Chadian war effort. With no money to speak of, the only asset he can donate is his son.

title: A Secret
synopsis: In 1953, a French boy finds out from a neighbour that his family is Jewish. An exploration of a dark family secret and the story of a passion, told through the interior journey of François, a solitary child who invents a brother and imagines his parents’ past.

title: A Short Film About Killing
synopsis: Jacek, an angry drifter, murders a taxi driver brutally and without motive. His case is assigned to Piotr, an idealistic young lawyer who is morally opposed to the death penalty, and their interactions take on an emotional honesty that throws into stark relief, for Piotr, the injustice of killing.

title: A Serious Man
synopsis: The story of an ordinary man’s search for clarity when his life begins to come apart at the seams. Struggling for equilibrium, he seeks advice from three different rabbis. Can anyone help him cope with his afflictions and become a righteous person, a mensch, a serious man?

title: A Separation
synopsis: When his wife leaves him, Nader hires a young woman to take care of his senile father. But he doesn’t know his new maid is not only pregnant but also working without her unstable husband’s permission. Soon, Nader finds himself entangled in a web of lies, manipulation, and public confrontations.

title: A Short Film About Love
synopsis: 19-year-old postal worker Tomek routinely spies on his older neighbor Magda, a sexually liberated artist who lives in the apartment across the courtyard from his. As their private worlds merge, fascination turns to obsession, and the line between love and curiosity becomes blurred.

title: A Short Story
synopsis: Once upon a time, there was a strange black cat in a long trenchcoat with no friends, no home, and no purpose in life. However, one day, an encounter with a scarecrow sets the black cat off on a mysterious quest to answer a single question: What is the most precious thing in the world?

title: A Single Man
synopsis: Mourning the loss of his partner Jim, George Falconer, an English professor working in Los Angeles, is finding life increasingly difficult to face. The series of events and encounters occurring over a single day will ultimately lead him to decide if there is a future for him in this world.

title: A Sip of Love
synopsis: A drama film featuring Kadir İnanır and Hale Soygazi as an unhappily married couple, who are divided when he loses his job and she is forced to find factory work. The film won 5 Golden Oranges at the 21st Antalya Golden Orange Film Festival.

title: A Slightly Pregnant Man
synopsis: Marco, an Italian driving instructor living in Paris, pays a visit to the doctor, who promptly diagnoses him as four months pregnant. The medical miracle is heard round the world, as Marco becomes fodder for the press, a curiosity to the scientific world, and a cash cow for a maternity-wear company.

title: A Star Is Born
synopsis: Rock musician Jackson Maine lives half his life on tour and the other half at the bottom of a bottle. The pressures of fame have isolated him, but a chance encounter with Ally and her amazing singing voice changes all that. However, as her career takes off, his internal demons begin to resurface…

title: A Special Day
synopsis: On a special day in 1938, all of Rome is attending a parade celebrating Hitler’s meeting with Mussolini. A working-class housewife, left alone to tend to household duties, meets the only person not attending the parade, a liberal radio broadcaster, and the two forge an unexpectedly close friendship.

title: A Still Small Voice
synopsis: An aspiring hospital chaplain begins a yearlong residency in spiritual care, only to discover that to successfully tend to her patients, she must look deep within herself.

title: A Strange Colour of Dream
synopsis: Ertil, a retired architect, spends his days designing 3D projects shaped by dreams of Turkey’s potential modernization. Outside, Istanbul continues to build itself layer upon layer. One day, he learns that the building he lives in is set to be demolished due to earthquake risk.

title: A Strange Path
synopsis: A young filmmaker returning to his hometown is caught off guard by the rapid onset of a pandemic and must find his father, whom he hasn’t spoken to in over ten years. After their first meeting, strange things begin to happen.

title: A Strange Sight To Behold: The Van Lake Monster
synopsis: Gaining mainstream attention in the 1990s, the Lake Van Monster became a lasting symbol of the city. This short documentary examines the phenomenon with a critical eye.

title: A Summer's Tale
synopsis: Graduate Gaspard goes on holiday to the seaside. He’s hoping his sort-of girlfriend Léna will join him there, but as time passes, he welcomes the interest of Margot. When Margot encourages him to date her friend Solène, he complies. But when Léna turns up, Gaspard will have to choose.

title: A Swedish Love Story
synopsis: Two teenagers meet and cautiously fall in love in beautiful surroundings during the peak of an idyllic Swedish summer. Oblivious to social boundaries, they innocently create their own inner world, expecting little from the dysfunctional and world-weary adults around them.

title: A Tale of Love and Desire
synopsis: When starting university, Ahmed meets Farah, a Tunisian girl who has just arrived in Paris. While discovering a corpus of sensual and erotic Arabic literature he never imagined existed, Ahmed falls deeply in love with Farah, and although literally overwhelmed with desire, he will try to resist it.

title: A Tale of Two Sisters
synopsis: Released from a mental institution, Su-mi returns home with her father and her younger sister, Su-yeon, only to face her cold stepmother’s strange behavior, and the disturbing apparitions that are haunting their house. What dark secret connects them all to the family’s past?

title: A Thousand and One
synopsis: After unapologetic and fiercely loyal Inez kidnaps her son Terry from the foster care system, mother and son set out to reclaim their sense of home, identity, and stability, in a rapidly changing New York City.

title: A Tiger in Paradise
synopsis: Musician José González has experienced several psychotic episodes—a state in which the line between fantasy and reality blurs. Determined never to return to those depths again, he adheres to strict routines immersing himself in scientific theories and composing the lyrics of his upcoming album.

title: A Touch of Sin
synopsis: Four people in different provinces are driven to violent ends: An angry miner is enraged by corruption in his village. A migrant discovers the possibilities of owning a firearm. A receptionist is pushed beyond her limits by an abusive client. A young factory worker goes from one job to the next.

title: A Traveler's Needs
synopsis: Iris, a woman who finds herself adrift in Seoul and without any means to make ends meet, turns to teaching French, albeit via a very peculiar method. As she navigates a series of encounters, details of Iris’ situation slowly emerge—but the mysteries surrounding her only deepen.

title: A Trip to the Moon
synopsis: Six scholars, members of the Astronomers’ Club, set off on an expedition to the moon. Traveling in a bullet-shaped rocket, these voyagers arrive to meet the moon’s inhabitants: the Selenites. Having to escape the Selenties’ king, the scholars will try to return to Earth in their rocket.

title: A Turkish Heart with a German Mind
synopsis: The story of people of Turkish origin who stand out in the culture and arts community in Germany. The documentary consists of interviews and works of well-known “Germanist” artists.

title: A Useful Ghost
synopsis: Worried about her husband being allergic to dust, Nat, a recently-dead woman, returns as a ghost possessing a vacuum cleaner to clean the house and protect her family from other vengeful ghosts in the house. To become a useful ghost, she needs to get rid of the useless ones.

title: ...A Valparaíso
synopsis: A melancholic narrator begins a tour up the steep hills and across the sea of Valparaíso, a Chilean port city that was once a central stop around Cape Horn. Now, the glories of a cosmopolitan past are a faint memory—and the contrasts between poor and rich are immediately evident.

title: A White Night
synopsis: İlhan, an introverted clerk, finds solace in talking to the city’s buildings. One night, as he engages in his peculiar ritual, Semiha, an elderly and ailing woman, notices him. Their chance encounter sets the stage for a magical night.

title: A White, White Day
synopsis: In a remote Icelandic town, an off-duty police chief begins to suspect a local man of having had an affair with his late wife, who died in a tragic accident two years earlier. Gradually his obsession for finding out the truth takes over his life and begins to endanger himself and his loved ones.

title: A Winter's Tale
synopsis: One summer, the young Felicie and Charles fall deeply, passionately in love. Five years later, after accidentally giving him a false address, she is raising his child and drifting back and forth between two infatuated men with whom she’s unwilling, or unable, to settle down.

title: A Woman Escapes
director: Sofia Bohdanowicz, Blake Williams, Burak Çevik
synopsis: Audrey Benac lives alone in Paris after having moved there to tend to the home of her recently deceased friend. She struggles to find her footing in the world, moving through the days without any clear motivation or sense of purpose.  Audrey forges a correspondence with two male filmmakers.

title: A Woman Is a Woman
synopsis: Exotic dancer Angela attempts to have a child with her unwilling lover Emile. But Alfred, Emile’s best friend who is in love with Angela, is happy to be a father. Angela loves Emile and refuses Alfred’s advances, but leads Emile to believe that she is infatuated with his friend.

title: A Woman Under the Influence
synopsis: Mabel, desperate and lonely, is married to a Los Angeles municipal construction worker, Nick. Increasingly unstable, especially around others, she craves happiness, but her volatile behaviour convinces Nick that she poses a danger to their family and decides to commit her to an institution.

title: Aaahh Belinda
synopsis: Serap, a young actress with a strong, lively personality and a special dislike for middle-class family life, takes part in a TV-commercial for a recently marketed shampoo, “Belinda”. She plays the role of a typical housewife called Naciye.

title: A Zed and Two Noughts
synopsis: A car collides with a swan outside a zoo. Two women passengers die but the driver Alba survives with a leg amputation. Obsessed with the accident, the zoologist husbands of the dead women—twins Oliver and Oswald—become fascinated by the processes of decay and embark on a strange affair with Alba.

title: Abouna
synopsis: Tahir and Amine, who live in N’djamena, wake up one morning to learn that their father has left home. The brothers go looking for him and end up hanging about and going to the movies. At the cinema, they see their father on the big screen and decide to steal the reel as a memory of their absent dad.

title: About Dry Grasses
synopsis: Disenchanted art teacher Samet longs to escape his quiet Anatolian village, wrestling with unfulfilled ambitions and accusations of misconduct at school. A budding friendship with Nuray, a charismatic fellow teacher, offers solace, but jealousy arises when her affection shifts to another colleague.

title: About Endlessness
synopsis: A couple floats over a war-torn Cologne. A father stops to tie his daughter’s shoelaces in the rain on the way to a birthday party. Meanwhile, teenage girls dance outside a café, and a defeated army marches to a prisoner-of-war camp. Lives past and present attempt to find meaning in the world.

title: About Love
synopsis: Three generations of the Phadke family live and work together in South Mumbai. As they prepare for a family wedding, director Archana Atul Phadke, who is not in any hurry to marry, observes the shifting, often very funny household dynamics.

title: Absent
synopsis: Mert, a 30-year-old male, is employed at an abandoned-looking amusement park. He is grappling with an internal duality, while sensing a lurking identity bent on undermining him.

title: Abuk Sabuk Bir Film
synopsis: Ademoğlu has never laughed in his life. Years ago, he saved a wealthy German from freezing, who in return left him a vast fortune. Now the richest man in the country, Ademoğlu remains incapable of laughter. A grand competition is announced: whoever can make him laugh will win a generous prize.

title: Accident
synopsis: Vain and insecure Oxford don Stephen’s life is thrown into turmoil when he falls in love with one of his students, Anna. But she has also attracted the attention of William, a wealthy student, and Charley, a colleague of whose professional success and self-confidence Stephen is profoundly envious.

title: Accattone
synopsis: On the mean streets of Rome, a pimp leads a hand-to-mouth existence on the very margins of society—soliciting women, scrounging around, and exploiting others. When his prize sex worker Maddalena is arrested and jailed, Accattone’s fortunes dwindle, and he is forced to confront his existence.

title: Ach, diese Lücke, diese entsetzliche Lücke
synopsis: Accepted into a prestigious acting school in Munich, Joachim moves into his grandparents’ grand villa, where eccentric rituals and heavy drinking shape daily life. As he navigates the competitive, performative world of theater, rehearsals and family habits fuel doubt, ambition, and a search for self

title: Across the Sea
synopsis: Damla, six months pregnant, lives in New York and has recently married the American Kevin. Damla and Kevin travel to Damla’s summer hometown, where she is reintroduced to her family and her first love, Burak, whose presence questions the comfortable American life she has worked so hard to achieve.

title: Ad Astra
synopsis: Astronaut Roy McBride travels to the outer edges of the solar system to find out the truth about his missing father and unravel a mystery that threatens the survival of our planet. His journey will uncover secrets that challenge the nature of human existence and our place in the cosmos.

title: Actual People
synopsis: There’s one week left before college ends, and Riley has a professor failing her, a roommate who wants her out, and a UTI. But after meeting Leo, a fellow Asian-American from her hometown who’s a great listener, Riley drops everything and heads back to Philadelphia to pursue this perfect man.

title: Adaptation.
synopsis: Charlie Kaufman is a confused L.A. screenwriter overwhelmed by feelings of inadequacy, sexual frustration, self-loathing, and the screenwriting ambitions of his freeloading twin brother Donald. While struggling to adapt the latest book by Susan Orlean, Kaufman’s life spins from pathetic to bizarre.

title: Adam and the Devil
synopsis: On the first day of Ramadan, Hasan arrives at a railway station in the middle of wilderness. He has with him a wife and a young daughter. He has heard that the small station community needs an imam to lead the prayer ritual and teach the children Koran during the holy month.

title: Adieu Philippine
synopsis: France, 1960. In two months, young technician Michel will leave for Algeria to fulfill his military duties. Until then, he spends his time dating best friends Liliane and Juliette. When he goes on holiday to Corsica, the girls join him, and a game of seduction and rivalry evolves between them.

title: Affliction
synopsis: In a small New Hampshire town, washed-up cop Wade Whitehouse has a troubled past and an unimpressive streak as a lawman. When hunting guide Jack Hewitt reports a businessman’s accidental death during a hunting trip, Wade has a chance to turn around his life, if he can prove it was murder.

title: Afire
synopsis: Self-important author Leon joins his best friend on a summer holiday near the Baltic Sea to complete his novel. When they arrive, they find their house is already occupied by a carefree woman who challenges Leon to open up. Meanwhile, forest wildfires rage around them and impending disaster looms.

title: After the Hunt
synopsis: At a prestigious university, a professor’s world unravels when a gifted student accuses one of her colleagues of misconduct. As tensions escalate, she must navigate a fractured campus while confronting a dark secret from her past that resurfaces, threatening her career, reputation, and identity.

title: After the Storm
synopsis: Dwelling on his past as an acclaimed author, Ryota wastes his money on gambling and can barely pay child support. After the death of his father, his family seem to be moving on with their lives. Ryota struggles to take back control of his existence and to find a lasting place in the life of his son.

title: After the Wedding
synopsis: Seeking funds for her orphanage in India, Isabelle travels to New York to meet Theresa, a potentially perfect benefactor. An invitation to attend a wedding ignites a series of events in which the past collides with the present as mysteries unravel.

title: Afternoon Delight
synopsis: A woman takes her husband to a strip club to spice up their marriage. After she gets a private dance from a sexy blond dancer, she invites the girl to work for her as a live-in nanny.

title: After Yang
synopsis: When his young daughter’s beloved companion—an android—malfunctions, a father searches for a way to repair him. In the process, he discovers the life that has been passing in front of him, reconnecting with his wife and daughter across a distance he didn’t know was there.

title: Aftersun
synopsis: At a fading vacation resort, 11-year-old Sophie treasures rare time together with her loving and idealistic father, Calum. Twenty years later, Sophie’s tender recollections of their last holiday become a powerful and heartrending portrait of their relationship.

title: Afterschool
synopsis: Robert is a student at an elite East Coast school who accidentally captures on camera the tragic death of two classmates. Their lives become memorialized as part of an audio-visual assignment designed to speed up the campus-wide healing process. But the video results in an atmosphere of paranoia.

title: Against the Tide
synopsis: Two friends, both Indigenous fishermen, are driven to desperation by a dying sea. Their friendship begins to fracture as they take very different paths to provide for their struggling families.

title: Age of Uprising: The Legend of Michael Kohlhaas
synopsis: In the 16th century in the Cévennes, a horse dealer leads a happy family life. When a lord treats him unjustly, he raises an army and puts the country to fire and sword in order to have his rights restored.

title: Agon
synopsis: Three elite athletes train for the Summer Games, Ludoj, 2024. Facing press attention, injury, and exhaustion, they practice their respective disciplines of rifle shooting, fencing, and judo. The women work with coaches to optimize their performance before competing under acute pressure at the Games.

title: Agrarian Utopia
synopsis: Follows two debt-ridden families in Thailand that are forced to grow rice on someone else’s land.

title: Aguirre, the Wrath of God
synopsis: The legend of El Dorado, a land rich in gold, tempts Gonzalo Pizarro to lead an expedition beyond the Andes in 1560. Forced to halt, two men are sent downriver with a patrol. When their rafts are damaged, one decides to return to the camp, while Aguirre leads a mutiny and takes charge of the patrol.

title: Ahed's Knee
synopsis: Y, an Israeli filmmaker, arrives in a remote village in the desert to present one of his films. There he meets Yahalom, an officer for the Ministry of Culture, and finds himself fighting two losing battles: One against the death of freedom in his country, the other against the death of his mother.

title: Ain't Nothin' Without You
synopsis: Falling in love after a meet-cute on Hamburg’s frozen-over Alster Lake, Martha—a filmmaker with guilt over her privilege—and stalled architecture student Alfred begin a lively back-and-forth on every topic du jour. Meanwhile, Martha cohabits with her nanny’s family to feel a sense of social reality.

title: Dreams
synopsis: The disparate lives of shell-shocked soldier Ali, medical student Mehdi and bride Ahlaam — whose fiancé was kidnapped by Saddam Hussein’s forces on their wedding day — converge in a psychiatric hospital.

title: Air
synopsis: Air reveals the game-changing partnership between a then-rookie Michael Jordan and Nike’s fledgling basketball division, which revolutionized the world of sports and contemporary culture with the Air Jordan brand.

title: Air Doll
synopsis: One day, a life-size, inflatable sex doll Hideo suddenly comes to life. Without her owner knowing, she walks around town and falls in love with Junichi. Hideo starts to date Junichi and gets a job at the same video store where he works. Everything seems to be perfect—until the unexpected happens.

title: A.K.
synopsis: Chris Marker’s documentary records the French filmmaker’s several days on location with Akira Kurosawa during the production of Ran. The result is a work of art on the making of a work of art–an ode to Kurosawa in which it is principally the details that attract Marker’s attention.

title: Alcarràs
synopsis: In a small Catalonian village, the peach farmers of the Solé family spend every summer together picking fruit from their orchard. But when plans arise to install solar panels and cut down trees, the members of this tight-knit group suddenly face eviction — and the loss of far more than their home.

title: Akira
synopsis: It’s 2019, thirty-one years after the Japanese government nuked Tokyo as damage control for an experiment involving paranormal abilities in children. Cyberpunk biker Kaneda rides into the unknown on a mission to save his friend Tetsuo from a vast and far-reaching government conspiracy.

title: Alexandra
synopsis: In a desolate corner of the world, an elderly woman has come to her beloved grandson, a young officer stationed at a remote military outpost, with the enemy just beyond.

title: Alan Vega, Just a Million Dreams
synopsis: Renowned visual artist Alan Vega tells unique stories from his experiences in the 1970s and 1980s when he was a pioneering figure in the minimalist electronic rock scene as the vocalist and composer for the punk/post-punk duo Suicide.

title: Alexander
synopsis: The King of Macedonia leads his legions against the giant Persian Empire. After defeating the Persians, he leads his army across the then known world, venturing farther than any westerner had ever gone, all the way to India.

title: Ali & Ava
synopsis: A shared love of music ignites sparks between Ali and Ava. Ali finds comfort in Ava’s warmth and kindness while Ava finds Ali’s complexity and humour irresistible. They have to find a way to keep their newfound passion being overshadowed by the stresses of their separate histories.

title: Ali: Fear Eats the Soul
synopsis: Emmi is lonely in her old age. Her husband died years ago, and her grown children offer little companionship. One night she goes to a bar and strikes up a friendship with middle-aged mechanic Ali. They soon fall in love, and their families and neighbors are outraged by their spontaneous marriage.

title: Alice in the Cities
synopsis: Phillip is a roving German reporter who, after a chance encounter with an elusive American woman, reluctantly accepts temporary custody of little Alice. Their friendship grows while traveling through various European cities on a search for the girl’s grandmother.

title: Alice
synopsis: A quiet young English girl named Alice finds herself in an alternate version of her own reality after chasing a white rabbit. She becomes surrounded by living inanimate objects and stuffed dead animals, and must find a way out of this nightmare, no matter how twisted or odd that way must be.

title: Alice in Wonderland
synopsis: In 1970s Argentina, young Alicia follows a harried man who resembles a white rabbit down an escalator into a surreal underworld that mirrors her own Buenos Aires—its downtown streets, parks, and beaches now strange and distorted.

title: Alix's Pictures
synopsis: The photographer Alix Cléo-Roubaud talks with Boris, the son of director Jean Eustache. She shows him a series of photographs and explains her intentions, the context, and the people depicted. Little by little, her commentary drifts away from the images…

title: All About My Mother
synopsis: Following the tragic death of her teenage son, Manuela travels from Madrid to Barcelona in an attempt to contact the long-estranged father the boy never knew. She reunites with an old friend, an outspoken transgender sex worker, and befriends a troubled actress and a young nun.

title: AliEN0089
synopsis: A gamer uploads a testimonial video to denounce the harassment she suffers in a video game. Meanwhile, a stranger enters her home and hacks her computer, blurring the boundaries between the real and virtual worlds with chaotic consequences.

title: All That Breathes
synopsis: In New Delhi, legions of birds fall from the skies as the city smolders with social unrest. Brothers Nadeem and Saud race to save a casualty of the turbulent times: the black kite, a majestic bird of prey essential to their city’s ecosystem, struggling to survive in Delhi’s contaminated environment.

title: Toxic Love
synopsis: A group of heroin users hanging out at Ostia beach in Rome spends their time squabbling among themselves as they relentlessly pursue their next score, fuelled by petty theft and armed robbery. This routine is only momentarily punctuated with the faint hope of getting clean.

title: Brainstorm
synopsis: Like every teenager, Neto enjoys small acts of rebellion. But he is misunderstood by his strict parents. Fearing they are losing control, they commit Neto to a psychiatric hospital, where he experiences an inhuman reality and undergoes emotions and horrors that he never imagined could exist.

title: Brand Upon the Brain!
synopsis: Guy and his sister grow up on an island. His domineering mother keeps watch over everything from the lighthouse, while his eccentric father experiments in the basement laboratory. Another pair of siblings, the Lightball Kids, are two detectives sent to the island to bring light into the darkness.

title: Bread and Roses
synopsis: In Los Angeles, undocumented Mexican immigrant Maya finds work through her sister, Rosa. After putting up with abuse from her bosses and grim work conditions, Maya is open to the pro-union rhetoric offered by organiser Sam Shapiro, but Rosa is less interested in clashing with management.

title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
synopsis: A former boxer-turned-drug runner finds himself in a gunfight between the police and his own ruthless allies. When the smoke clears, he is badly hurt and thrown in prison, where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence that turn the place into a savage battleground.

title: Breaker Morant
synopsis: At the turn of the twentieth century, three Australian officers are court-martialed for alleged war crimes. With no time to prepare, an Australian major, appointed as defense attorney, must prove that they were just following orders and are being turned into political pawns.

title: Breath
synopsis: Yeon, a passionate and lonely artist, is intrigued to learn that Jiang Jin is awaiting execution on death row for the murder of his wife and children. She decides to visit him in prison, and their relationship evolves into a dreamy yet violent passion.

title: Breaking the Waves
synopsis: In a small coastal town in Scotland, Bess, a religious young woman, falls in love with a charming oil rig worker. Shortly after they marry, an unfortunate work accident cripples him for life, which shatters Bess’s world completely—especially when he encourages her to take on other lovers.

title: Breath
synopsis: Tamer, reliant on friends to navigate life, Aslı, aimless and living with family while tending to a sick neighbor, and Celil, an ordinary caregiver emotionally detached but devoted to his patients until death, cross paths in a poignant exploration of interdependence and survival.

title: Breathing
synopsis: Roman is serving time in a juvenile detention center.  After half of his term, Roman is released on probation and, after many failed attempts, he finds a job at the morgue in Vienna. One day, Roman is faced with a dead woman who bears his family name, and he begins to wonder about his past.

title: Breathless
synopsis: Petty thug Michel panics and impulsively kills a policeman while driving a stolen car. On the lam, he turns to his aspiring journalist girlfriend Patricia, hiding out in her Paris apartment. When she learns that Michel is being investigated for murder, Patricia begins to question her loyalty.

title: Breathless
synopsis: Gangster Soon-Hong’s life is a cycle of violence and self-loathing. When he meets schoolgirl Han Yeon-Heui, he not only meets his match, but his possible salvation. Is his lifestyle one he can transcend?

title: Bright Star
synopsis: At the start of the 19th century, young Fanny Brawne meets aloof poet John Keats. She, insolent and frivolous, likes to shine in society when he, a penniless poet, is entirely up to the demands of artistic creation. Despite their differences, an intense and delicate passion blossoms between them.

title: Bright Young Things
synopsis: In 1930s England, a group of reckless socialites dominate national gossip. Among them, aspiring novelist Adam is trying to raise enough money to marry Nina. While his attempts are constantly thwarted, his friends are slowly on the road to destruction in their search for newer and faster sensations.

title: Bring Her Back
synopsis: A brother and sister uncover a terrifying ritual at the secluded home of their new foster mother.

title: Bring Them Down
synopsis: Michael, the last son of a shepherding family, lives with his ailing father, Ray. When a conflict with rival farmer Gary and his son Jack escalates, a devastating chain of events leaves both families permanently altered.

title: Broadway by Light
synopsis: Broadway, New York City, 1950s. The flashing bulbs and neons of Times Square adverts and the silhouettes of men at work on theatre marquees re-arranging letters on the lightboxes—poised like acrobats on their stepladders—create a kaleidoscopic light show.

title: Broken Memories
synopsis: An audiovisual chronicle of the Spanish Civil War in Galicia. Memorias Rotas centers on a group of republican fighters leaded by Commander José Moreno. The group disappears as they fail trying to escape by sea in the border between Galicia and Asturias and nobody ever knows about them.

title: Broker
synopsis: On a rainy night in Busan, So-young leaves her baby Woo-sung outside a ‘baby box’, a safe place set up in Korean churches for new mothers to leave unwanted infants. Instead, he’s picked up by Sang-hyun who runs an unofficial adoption brokerage and plans to find him a new home.

title: Bronson
synopsis: Michael Peterson always dreamed of being famous. Sentenced to 7 years in prison for robbing a post office with a makeshift army, he ended up spending 34 years, including 30 in solitary confinement. He is now known as Charles Bronson, aka “Britain’s most violent prisoner”.

title: Broken Land
synopsis: In a desert landscape, a small community of Americans lives in the shade of the wall erected to protect them from illegal immigrants.

title: I Used to Be Funny
synopsis: An aspiring stand-up comedian and au pair struggling with PTSD, as she decides whether or not to join the search for a missing teenage girl she used to nanny.

title: Brother
synopsis: Abandoned by the brotherhood of his yakuza clan, tough guy Yamamoto is forced to leave Tokyo. He goes to Los Angeles in search of Ken, his younger half-brother. Alone and with a new identity, Yamamoto finds himself frustrated by foreign surroundings, especially since he doesn’t speak the language.

title: Brother Number One
synopsis: The New Zealander and Olympian rowing champion Rob Hamill travels to Cambodia to seek justice for his eldest brother Kerry, who disappeared without a trace while sailing from Australia to Southeast Asia in 1978. Along with two sailing mates, Kerry was murdered by the Khmer Rouge regime.

title: Brothers of the Night
synopsis: Soft boys by day, kings by night. Young Bulgarian Roma who come to Vienna looking for freedom and a quick buck. They sell their bodies as if that’s all they had. What comforts them, so far from home, is the feeling of being together. But the nights are long and unpredictable.

title: Brownian Movement
synopsis: Charlotte and Max live in Brussels with their son. The couple hits a crisis when Max discovers that Charlotte has been having short but intense affairs with her patients. Charlotte goes into therapy and has to give up her job as a doctor, and the pair try to start their lives over together.

title: Bye Bye Brazil
synopsis: A motley crew of traveling performers on the Caravana Rolidei rolls into various towns across Brazil’s northwestern Amazonian landscape, entertaining locals who still have no access to television. Accordionist Ciço and his wife Dasdô join the lively caravan, leading to a string of adventures.

title: Chamada a Cobrar
synopsis: Clarinha is a wealthy lady who enjoys a comfortable life in the city of São Paulo. The peace and tranquility of this upper-middle-class woman ends when she answers a phone call from an alleged kidnapper who has kidnapped her daughter.

title: Critico
synopsis: Seventy critics and filmmakers discuss cinema around the conflict between the artist and the observer, the creator and the critic. Between 1998 and 2007, Kleber Mendonça Filho recorded testimonies about this relationship in Brazil, the United States and Europe, based on his experience as a critic.

title: Crossing the Bridge - The Sound of Istanbul
synopsis: German musician Alexander Hacke sets out with his mobile recording studio to capture the musical diversity of Istanbul and construct a portrait of Turkish music. His journey leads to the discovery of a broad spectrum ranging from rock and classical arabesque to modern electronic and hip-hop.

title: De Humani Corporis Fabrica
synopsis: Five centuries ago, anatomist André Vésale opened up the body to science for the first time in history. Today, human flesh is revealed as a landscape that exists only through others’ attention. As places of care, suffering and hope, hospitals are laboratories that connect every body in the world.

title: Death of a Unicorn
synopsis: While driving to a remote retreat, Ridley and her father Elliot accidentally hit a unicorn with their car. They bring the body to the woodland estate of a billionaire pharmaceutical CEO, where curiosity turns to chaos as the mythical creature’s existence sparks corporate and personal agendas.

title: Embrace of the Serpent
synopsis: Karamakate is a warrior shaman and the last of his tribe who lives alone in the Colombian Amazon. Only he knows how to find the Yakruna—a rare, psychedelic plant. Two scientists—at two different times, with very different agendas—enlist Karamakate on their quests to find this mythical plant.

title: Enrico's Birthday
synopsis: December 1999. Amid the Millennium Bug anxiety, a boy attends the birthday of Enrico, a kid who lives with his family in an old and isolated farmhouse.

title: Father Mother Sister Brother
synopsis: A feature film in the form of a triptych. The three stories all concern the relationships between adult children, their somewhat distant parent (or parents), and each other.

title: Foxy Brown
synopsis: Foxy Brown has found her soul mate in an undercover narcotics investigator, but when he is brutally murdered, she vows vengeance against the crime ring responsible and takes a job as a high-society sex worker to infiltrate the gang.

title: God's Gift
synopsis: A peddler crossing the savanna discovers a child lying unconscious in the bush. When the boy comes to, he is mute and cannot explain who he is. Wend Kuuni regains his speech only after witnessing a tragic event that prompts him to reveal his own painful history.

title: Gods of Mexico
synopsis: In rural Mexico, resistance to modernization showcases the diversity of Native Peoples and Afro-descendant communities, honoring daily labor and preserving cultural identity. It explores a “Lost Atlantis” where diverse possibilities resist the rapid march of progress.

title: In Fabric
synopsis: A lonely divorcee visits a bewitching London department store in search of a dress that will transform her life. She’s fitted with a perfectly flattering, artery-red gown—which, in time, will come to unleash a malevolent curse and unstoppable evil, threatening everyone who comes into its path.

title: Jeanne Dielman, 23, Quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles
synopsis: Jeanne Dielman is a middle-aged widow whose daily routine of chores includes making the beds, cooking dinner for her teenage son, and some occasional sex work. The rhythm and the rites of her every day are unchangeable. But when something changes, her routine slowly begins to unravel.

title: Jobriath A.D.
synopsis: Called “The American Bowie,” “The True Fairy of Rock & Roll” and “Hype of the Year,” Jobriath’s reign as the first openly gay rock star was brief and over by 1975. Now, 35 years later, Jobriath A.D. spotlights his life, music, groundbreaking influence.

title: Let the Corpses Tan
synopsis: During a Mediterranean summer, Rhino and his gang steal a cache of gold and hide in an abandoned hamlet occupied by a woman seeking inspiration. However, surprise guests and two cops disrupt their plan, turning the once-heavenly retreat of wild happenings into a gruesome battlefield.

title: Leila's Brothers
synopsis: At 40 years old, Leila has spent her whole life taking care of her parents and her four brothers. The family argues constantly and is crushed by debts, in a country caught in the grip of international economic sanctions. As her brothers are trying to make ends meet, Leila formulates a plan.

title: Licorice Pizza
synopsis: It was 1973 in the San Fernando Valley, and 15-year-old child actor Gary Valentine found himself falling hard for the free-spirited, 10-years-older Alana Kane. As he looks to impress her with entrepreneurial hustle and innate chutzpah, her bemused interest may give way to something more.

title: Marina Abramović: The Artist is Present
synopsis: Seductive, fearless, and outrageous, Marina Abramovic has been redefining what art is for nearly forty years. Using her body as a vehicle, pushing herself beyond her physical and mental limits––and at times risking her life in the process––she creates performances that challenge, shock, and move us.

title: Men on the Bridge
synopsis: The stories of three men working at the Bosphorus Bridge in Istanbul are told by the original characters, in this mosaic depicting real persons exposing their lives and aspirations.

title: Metrobranding: A Love Story Between Men and Objects
synopsis: While people in Western Europe were used to choose between hundreds of brands, the communist Romania used to have a different experience: one brand for each basic product. Imagine the importance these “monobrands” acquired in the lives of those who made them and who used them.

title: Misericordia
synopsis: Out-of-work Jérémie drifts back to his hometown after the death of his beloved former boss, a bakery owner. Staying long after the funeral, the seemingly benign Jérémie casually insinuates himself into his mentor’s family, including his kind-hearted widow and venomously angry son.

title: Museum of Broken Love
synopsis: Ali starts working in the Museum of Broken Love, a museum where belongings from people’s past relationships are exhibited. He’s in charge of receiving the belongings and placing them in the displays. He begins filling his lonely life with the happy fantasies he imagines over these objects.

title: Nico, 1988
synopsis: Approaching 50, singer-songwriter Nico leads a solitary existence, far from her glam days as a Warhol superstar and vocalist for cult band The Velvet Underground. A new manager convinces Nico to hit the road again. Meanwhile, she struggles with her demons and the consequences of a muddled life.

title: Point Break
synopsis: In Los Angeles, a gang of bank robbers called The Ex-Presidents commit their crimes while wearing masks of Reagan, Carter, Nixon and Johnson. The FBI believes that the members of the gang could be surfers, and send young agent Johnny Utah undercover to mix with them and gather information.

title: Post Tenebras Lux
synopsis: Juan, his wife, and his children live deep in the countryside of Mexico. As his relationship with his wife strains and he grapples with old memories and violent urges, a devil stalks the land and a horrific crime slowly brews…

title: Rocco and His Brothers
synopsis: Joining the exodus of millions from Italy’s impoverished south, a matriarch and her sons move to Milan in search of a better life in the industrial north. But, as they inch up the social ladder, family bonds are shredded, and saintly Rocco’s love for a sex worker drives his brutish brother to crime.

title: Santo vs. The Evil Brain
synopsis: A secret police agent is turned into a zombified slave by a mad scientist using a mind-control machine. To stop him, Lt. Zambrano creates a masked vigilante, Incógnito, who must restore Santo’s true self and foil the evil plans of the criminal mastermind, the Evil Brain.

title: Serpico
synopsis: Frank Serpico is a NYC cop who refuses to take bribes, unlike the rest of the force. His actions get Frank shunned by the other officers, and often placed in risky situations by his partners. When his superiors ignore Frank’s accusations of corruption, he decides to go public with the allegations.

title: sex, lies and videotape
synopsis: Ann, a frustrated wife, enters into counseling due to a troubled marriage. Unbeknownst to her, her husband John has begun an affair with her sister. When John’s best friend Graham arrives, his penchant for interviewing women about their sex lives forever changes John and Ann’s rocky marriage.

title: Sorrowful Shadow
synopsis: Widow Paramo has lost her husband to the plague. Their daughter Dolores is considering suicide, with El Muerto—the eater of souls— preparing himself to welcome her into the darkness. Paramo must triumph over Death in the boxing ring if she wishes to save both her husband and daughter.

title: Songs My Brothers Taught Me
synopsis: High school senior Johnny is about to leave the Pine Ridge Reservation when his rodeo-cowboy father suddenly dies. Reluctant to leave his 13-year-old sister behind, Johnny must face an inner conflict standing in the way of his departure, which leads both of them to rediscover the meaning of home.

title: Spring Breakers
synopsis: Four childhood friends land in jail after robbing a restaurant to fund their spring break vacation. They are bailed out by a rapper who promises to provide all the thrill and excitement they could hope for. But how far are they willing to go to experience a spring break they will never forget?

title: The Braves
synopsis: Dreaming, falling, starting over. Margot and Alma are two best friends holding on to the energy of their youth and their burning desire to conquer the world, until life gets in the way. But their ride-or-die friendship can get them through anything: they are inseparable, unstoppable.

title: The Bread and Alley
synopsis: Shot in black and white, the film tells the story of a little boy walking home with a loaf of bread who is confronted by a hungry dog, placing his safety at risk.

title: The Brickmakers
synopsis: Amid Bogotá’s urban estates, pottery workers face inhumane exploitation by employers. The intricate connections between technological advancements, production relations, and the ideological framework within this community of laborers, reveal the deep-seated issues that perpetuate their struggle.

title: The Brood
synopsis: Frank is becoming concerned about his wife’s secretive treatment at the Institute of Psychoplasmics. The doctor’s controversial methods unleash his patients’ rage, taking on physical manifestations. Frank investigates the institute while a series of brutal attacks are committed by mutant children.

title: The Broken Circle Breakdown
synopsis: Elise and Didier fall in love at first sight. They bond over their shared enthusiasm for American music and culture, and dive headfirst into a sweeping romance that plays out on and off stage—but when an unexpected tragedy hits their new family, everything they know and love is tested.

title: The Brutalist
synopsis: Escaping postwar Europe, a visionary architect comes to America to rebuild his life, his career, and his marriage. On his own in a strange new country, he settles in Pennsylvania, where a wealthy and prominent industrialist recognises his talent.

title: The Day Brazil Was Here
synopsis: Following Haiti’s 2004 coup, the UN deployed Brazilian troops to restore order. In a symbolic shift, football replaced weapons: Brazil’s national team played a historic friendly in Port-au-Prince, using the sport as a peace tool to soothe the nation’s violent internal conflicts.

title: The Lovers on the Bridge
synopsis: On an under-construction Pont Neuf, Paris’s oldest bridge, a hard-drinking street performer and a post-relationship young painter with failing eyesight live as vagrants. As her vision deteriorates, she becomes increasingly dependent on him, and they manage to fall in love amid their daily strife.

title: The Marriage of Maria Braun
synopsis: The Marriage of Maria Braun  is the “German Hollywood film” Fassbinder had long dreamed of making. A heartbreaking study of a woman picking herself up from the ruins of her own life, as well as a pointed metaphorical attack on a society determined to forget its past.

title: The Sisters Brothers
