synopsis: Fenyang, 1999: Tao has two suitors, Zhang and his best friend Liangzi. Tao decides, with a note of regret, to marry Zhang, an aspiring entrepreneur. Flash-forward 15 years, the couple’s son Dollar is paying a visit to his now-estranged mother and everyone has grown more distant in time and space.

title: Moving
synopsis: Renko, a spirited sixth-grader in Kyoto, struggles to accept her parents’ separation. When her father leaves, her mother sets new rules Renko can’t make sense of. In a bid to hold on to their old life, she hides the divorce papers and plans a getaway to a lake they once visited as a family.

title: Mr. Kaplan
synopsis: After fleeing Europe for Uruguay during WWII, Jacob Kaplan built a quiet life. Now 76, he begins to question his worth. After learning of a mysterious German prowling the shores of a nearby beach, he becomes convinced that he’s found a Nazi in hiding and plans to expose him.

title: Mr. Klein
synopsis: In Occupied Paris, Mr. Klein, a dispassionate art dealer, exploits the situation of the Jews fleeing from France by buying and selling their works of art. When he learns that the Nazi authorities suspect him of being Jewish, he seeks out the identity of a shadowy double.

title: Mrs Harris Goes to Paris
synopsis: A new adaptation of Paul Gallico’s much-loved novel about a cleaning lady in 1950s London who falls in love with an haute couture dress by Christian Dior and decides to gamble everything for the sake of this folly.

title: Mrs. Robinson
synopsis: A constitutional lawyer and senator in her early career, Mary Robinson detonated an electoral earthquake by winning the Irish Presidential vote in 1990. Later, as a UN High Commissioner, she built a lasting legacy; fearlessly challenging perpetrators of human rights abuses all over the world.

title: Mrs. Salkım’s Diamonds
synopsis: Durmuş and his wife Nimet immigrate from an Anatolian town, Niğde to Istanbul to have a better life and they start to live with Bekir. Bekir is an old friend of Durmuş from motherland and works as an attendant in a hostelry which belongs to Mr. Halit…

title: Much Loved
synopsis: In present-day Marrakech, Noha, Randa, Soukaina, and Hlima make a living through sex work. Living together, the friends navigate wild parties, corrupt police, and exploitative pimps—overcoming the violence of a society that takes advantage of them while at the same time condemning them.

title: Mud
synopsis: Ellis and his friend Neckbone are two boys who meet a fugitive hiding on an island in the Mississippi. Skeptical but intrigued by his fantastic scenarios, Ellis and Neckbone agree to help him.

title: Mulheres: Uma outra história
synopsis: Footage from 1986 to 1988 shows crowds at government assemblies and street protests campaigning for women’s rights. Often making direct appeals to the filmmaker, people speak of democratic processes, new legislation, and how some—yet not all—women are able to participate increasingly in public life.

title: Mulholland Drive
synopsis: Blonde Betty Elms has only just arrived in Hollywood to become a movie star when she meets an enigmatic brunette with amnesia. Meanwhile, as the two set off to solve the second woman’s identity, filmmaker Adam Kesher runs into ominous trouble while casting his latest project.

title: Mundane History
synopsis: Ake returns to his family home after an accident leaves him paralyzed from the waist down. A male nurse, Pun, is hired to care for Ake, whose hatred of his father has now grown into a hatred of the world at large. Friendship slowly build between them, and Ake begins to discover a new peace within.

title: Murder in Mississippi
synopsis: Torn-from-the-headlines exploitation or daring social commentary? Joseph P. Mawra’s film is a little of both, using the true story of the killing of civil rights workers in the American deep South as a gritty springboard for another ultra-low-budget entry from the director of Chained Girls.

title: Murder on the Orient Express
synopsis: After wrapping a case, detective Hercule Poirot anticipates a tranquil journey on the Orient Express. However, a murder of an unpopular billionaire occurs on board, making everyone a suspect. Poirot utilizes an avalanche to his advantage and uncovers revenge motives among passengers.

title: Muriel, or The Time of Return
synopsis: In Boulogne-sur-Mer, the past of widowed antique shop owner Hélène comes back to haunt her when a former lover reenters her life. Meanwhile, her stepson Bernard is tormented by his own ghosts, related to his service in France’s recently ended war in Algeria.

title: Muriel's Wedding
synopsis: Muriel finds her life dull and spends her days alone in her room listening to ABBA music and dreaming of her wedding day. Slight problem, Muriel has never had a date. After stealing some money, she decides to go on a tropical vacation and befriends the wacky Rhonda who turns her world upside down.

title: Murina
synopsis: On a bright, beautiful Croatian island, tensions rise between restless teenager Julija and her oppressive father Ante when an old family friend arrives at their home. Their tranquil yet isolated existence leaves Julija wanting more from this influential visitor, who offers a taste of liberation.

title: Musa
synopsis: One day, director Zeki Demirkubuz stumbles upon a pirate film seller and sees that his own films are also being sold. Demirkubuz will make an offer to Musa that can change his life.

title: Muse
synopsis: A struggle for power between a pianist and his unruly muse turns murderous.

title: Mutant Aliens
synopsis: Cult icon Bill Plympton dishes out his fourth foray into feature-length animation with this outrageous send-up of outer space adventures.

title: Mutt
synopsis: Over the course of a single hectic day in New York City, three people from Feña’s past are thrust back into his life. Having lost touch since transitioning from female to male, he navigates the new dynamics of old relationships while tackling the day-to-day challenges of living life in between.

title: My Blueberry Nights
synopsis: Following a nasty breakup, Elizabeth leaves her memories behind as she chases her dreams across the country. Picking up a series of jobs to support herself along the way, the myriad of lost souls she meets help her to understand the journey as part of a greater exploration within herself.

title: My Child
synopsis: What happens when your child comes out to you? In this feature documentary, parents of lesbian, gay, bisexual and trans-gender individuals in Turkey intimately share their experiences with the viewer, as they redefine what it means to be parents in this conservative society.

title: My Dear Enemy
synopsis: My Dear Enemy follows a pair of former lovers—he’s a charismatic romantic and she’s a no-nonsense realist—who bump into each other one year down the road. She wants back a hunk of cash she lent him, so they embark on a road trip to borrow small amounts from various acquaintances.

title: My Father's Shadow
synopsis: Over the course of a single day’s journey into and around Lagos, Folarin and his young sons, Akin and Remi, reckon with their relationship while navigating a city on the precipice of democratic crisis. As details of Folarin’s life emerge, the strain in their connection becomes increasingly clear.

title: My Favourite Cake
synopsis: The 70-year-old Mahin lives alone in Tehran, until she decides to break her solitary routine and revitalise her love life. But as she opens herself up to a new romance, an unexpected encounter quickly evolves into an unpredictable, unforgettable evening.

title: My First Film
synopsis: A young filmmaker, Vita, revisits her first chaotic attempt at filmmaking 15 years prior. Shooting a semi-autobiographical film starring her friend Dina, Vita’s eager but inexperienced approach causes the production to spiral into chaos, leading to significant disruptions and a near-fatal accident.

title: My Golden Days
synopsis: As he prepares to leave Tajikistan, anthropologist Paul Dédalus reflects on three moments from his youth. First, his childhood in Roubaix, then a school trip to the USSR. Finally, he recalls university life in Paris and partying in his hometown. Most of all he remembers Esther, the love of his life.

title: My Life Part 2
synopsis: Based on objects, photos, sound and film recordings, filmmaker Angelika Levi narrates what is—and isn’t—talked about in her family. Going through an extensive biographical archive, Levi uncovers the complicated life of her mother, Ursula, a woman of Jewish-German heritage who survived Nazi rule.

title: My Little Loves
synopsis: After spending years living with his grandma, Daniel joins his mother in the South of France. Living modestly as a seamstress, she is unable to afford his high school education and sends him to work as a mechanic’s apprentice. He begins to discover desire and embarks on his sentimental journey.

title: Ayka
synopsis: Ayka is a young Kyrgyz girl that lives and works illegally in Moscow. After giving birth to her son she leaves him in hospital as she cannot afford to raise a child. Some time later, however, her motherly yearning leads her to desperate attempts of finding the abandoned child.

title: My Little Sister
synopsis: Lisa, once a brilliant playwright, no longer writes, and lives with her family in Switzerland—though her heart remains in Berlin. When her twin brother, Sven, a theater actor, suffers from a leukemia, the relationship between them becomes closer as Lisa reflects on her desire to be creative again.

title: My Mother Learns Cinema
synopsis: In a cozy setting, Turkish director Nesimi Yetik, playing himself, imparts film history lessons to his mother, focusing on pronunciation of famed auteurs, revealing a unique mother-son dynamic.

title: My Mother's Girlfriend
synopsis: Renuka and Sadiya, two working-class women in love with each other, enjoy their day out celebrating Renuka’s birthday. After a fun-filled day, they head home to spend the night together. Unknown to them, Renuka’s son Mangesh is around.

title: My Name Is Alfred Hitchcock
synopsis: 2022 marks the hundred-year anniversary of Alfred Hitchcock’s first feature. A century on, Hitchcock remains one of the most influential filmmakers in the history of cinema. But how does his vast body of work and legacy hold up in today’s society?

title: My Name is Annemarie Schwarzenbach
synopsis: A biopic about Annemarie Schwarzenbach, who was a shimmering figure of bohemian society of the 1920s. A talented writer, she was lesbian, addicted to drugs, a globetrotter, bewitchingly androgynous and – much to her domineering Nazi-loving mother’s chagrin – also anti-fascist.

title: My Name Is Kahentiiosta
synopsis: 1990. A young Kahnawake Mohawk woman named Kahentiiosta is arrested after the Oka Crisis’ 78-day armed standoff. She is detained four days longer than the other women. Her crime? The prosecutor representing the Quebec government did not accept her Indigenous name.

title: My Neighbor Totoro
synopsis: Satsuki and Mei’s mother has taken ill. To be closer to her while she recovers, their father moves the sisters from their home in the city to a neglected rural house that sits in the shadow of an ancient tree. Together, the girls embark on adventures with the wondrous local forest spirits.

title: My Night at Maud's
synopsis: Over several days around Christmas, a pious Catholic engineer explores the intersection of chance and choice in life. After spotting the delicate Françoise at Mass, he vows to marry her, but all changes when he spends the night at the apartment of bold divorcée Maud on a snowy Christmas Eve.

title: My Old School
synopsis: In 1993, 16-year-old Brandon Lee enrolled at Bearsden Academy, a school in a suburb of Glasgow, Scotland. Brandon quickly succeeded in school, finding friends, taking bullied students under his wing, and even starring in school theater productions. But then his unbelievable secret was revealed.

title: My Only Sunshine
synopsis: 14-year-old Hayat, no longer a child but not yet a woman, lives with her father and grandfather by the shores of the Bosphorus in Istanbul. Her father works as a fisherman but makes most of his money through shady dealings. Even more corrosive than the family’s poverty is their neglect toward Hayat.

title: My Skin, Luminous
synopsis: Having lost the pigment in his skin, Matias, an infirmed orphan at a Michoacán primary school, has been quarantined from his classmates. Meanwhile, the presence and words of novelist Mario Bellatin offer the prospect of healing to his ailing body.

title: My Suburban Stories
synopsis: In the slums of Adana Ceyhan, Turkey, the residents share their unique tales. Culluk Yusuf, the bird-stealing master; Uncle Pele, the suit-changing eccentric; and Naime, the feminist village headman, among others, reveal their extraordinary lives filled with struggle, humor, and community.

title: My Summer with Irène
synopsis: Italy, 1997. Clara and Irene, both 17, meet at a summer camp organized by the hospital that treats them—they instantly become inseparable. Instead of returning to their families, they run away to a faraway island off the coast of Sicily to live their first summer as carefree young women.

title: My Uncle
synopsis: The story of a man who realizes his passion for flying one day and descends into the desert 42 years later, as told through the words of his nephew.

title: My Winnipeg
synopsis: The geographical dead center of North America and the beloved birthplace of Guy Maddin, Winnipeg, is the frosty and mysterious star of Maddin’s film. Fact, fantasy and memory are woven seamlessly together in this work, conjuring a city as delightful as it is fearsome.

title: Mysterious Skin
synopsis: Brian Lackey went missing for five hours when he was eight, an event he’s convinced was an alien abduction. In the same small Kansas town, teenage hustler Neil McCormick is a confused outsider with a dark past. Both young men pursue a self-destructive path that will lead them toward each other.

title: Mystery Train
synopsis: A rock ’n roll-obsessed Japanese couple pays a visit to Graceland, the home of Elvis. An inept gang of thugs hide out after a liquor store robbery. A frazzled Italian widow has a ghostly encounter with the King himself. All their stories collide in one sleazy downtown motel in Memphis, Tennessee.

title: Naked
synopsis: Johnny is a charming and eloquent, but relentlessly vicious, drifter. Rejecting anyone who might care for him, the volcanic Johnny hurls himself around London on a nocturnal odyssey, colliding with a succession of other desperate and dispossessed people and scorching everyone in his path.

title: Naked Lunch
synopsis: Bill Lee is a part-time exterminator and full-time drug addict, who uses insecticides to get high, plunging into a nightmarish interzone of sinister cabals and giant talking bugs. Believing himself to be a government agent, he flees to a secret organization after accidentally killing his wife.

title: Nam June Paik: Moon is the Oldest TV
synopsis: Nam June Paik was one of the founding fathers of avant-garde art in the 20th century. Performance footage and original interviews from Paik’s peers trace how the Korean artist became a pillar of the American art world and transformed modern image-making with his sculptures, films, and performances.

title: Nana
synopsis: In a remote stretch of the French countryside, 4-year-old Nana lives with her mother. When Nana returns home one day to an empty house, her mother mysteriously absent, a journey into the night of her childhood begins.

title: Nanny
synopsis: Piecing together a new life in New York City while caring for the child of an Upper East Side family, immigrant nanny Aisha is forced to confront a concealed truth that threatens to shatter her precarious American Dream.

title: Napoléon
synopsis: This epic biopic of Napoleon traverses many of the formative experiences that shaped the French leader’s rapid advancement: from his days in school to his flight from Corsica, as well as the French Revolution and the Terror, and culminating in his triumphant invasion of Italy in 1797.

title: Napoleon
synopsis: Based on the true story of Napoleon Bonaparte. This film looks at the military commander’s origins and his swift, ruthless climb to emperor, viewed through the prism of his addictive and often volatile relationship with his wife and one true love, Josephine.

title: Napoli, Napoli, Napoli
synopsis: A raw portrait of Naples, where social workers and politicians cannot get to grips with the underbelly of urban society—which makes the Camorra’s job all the easier.

title: Naz + Maalik
synopsis: Naz and Maalik are friends, classmates, business partners, and lovers. As the two closeted Muslim teens go about their regular daily routine on a Friday afternoon in Brooklyn, they arouse the suspicions of an undercover FBI agent who begins to track them.

title: Neds
synopsis: Set in 1970s Glasgow, this film tells the story of a shy and intelligent young boy who, through a series of circumstances, turns into a NED—a non-educated delinquent. Attending a new school, he becomes increasingly violent and aggressive, all the while searching for a way out.

title: Neige
synopsis: In Paris’ Pigalle, Anita, a barmaid with a big heart, lives among dealers and police. When Bobby, a young heroin dealer she cared for, is killed by the police, a shortage hits the neighborhood. Anita becomes involved in the drug network to help the users around her survive the crisis.

title: Neighbouring Sounds
synopsis: In Recife, on the Brazilian coast, residents of a flourishing neighborhood go about their quiet lives until their routines are disrupted by the arrival of a private security company. The company’s presence brings a sense of safety to some and tension to others, as power dynamics gradually emerge.

title: Nénette and Boni
synopsis: Nenette and Boni are estranged siblings. Boni works as a pizza chef and fantasizes about the baker’s wife, while Nenette is trapped in a boarding school. But when she unexpectedly shows up pregnant with a child she doesn’t want, Boni cannot hold his grudge much longer.

title: Nepal Forever
synopsis: A documentary comedy. This film features two Russian communist politicians. Their horizons are broad, but what concerns them the most is the future of global communism. At one point, by decree of fate, their booming activities begin to expand far beyond the boundaries of the Russian Federation.

title: Neptune Frost
synopsis: In Burundi, a group of escaped coltan miners form an anti-colonialist hacker collective, attempting a takeover of the regime that is exploiting the region’s natural resources. When an intersex runaway and a miner find each other, their connection sparks glitches within the greater divine circuitry.

title: Neredesin
synopsis: A farewell portrait built around the late artist Tardu Kuman’s sculptures and the voices that seem to emanate from them. An elegy rendered through sound and form.

title: Network
synopsis: When a veteran anchorman is forced out of his post, he announces to viewers that he will kill himself during his farewell broadcast. Executives rethink their decision when his fanatical tirade results in a spike in ratings.

title: Neukölln Spiderman
synopsis: Facing the risk of deportation from Germany, Altay learns that he must present a valid reason to the immigration office in order to stay. On his way home, he encounters a man wearing a Spider-Man mask and, curious about his own valid reason, conducts a brief interview.

title: Never Gonna Snow Again
synopsis: The Eastern European masseur Zhenia possesses an almost magical gift. Working in a gated community in Poland, he quickly becomes a guru-like figure to his wealthy clients. However, Zhenia’s background remains a mystery—leaving the residents to wonder what secrets he might be carrying with him.

title: New Jerusalem
synopsis: Sean, an Irish immigrant to America, returns from military service in Afghanistan, finds his heart and mind in disarray. He begins work at a tire shop, where he befriends Ike, a strong-willed Evangelical Christian, who endeavors to ensure the fragile Seanʼs salvation.

title: New Order
synopsis: In contemporary Mexico City, a lavish high society wedding is interrupted by violent rioters who take the house by siege. It soon becomes apparent that this seemingly random attack is part of a violent, nationwide uprising, as one political system collapses and a more harrowing replacement arises.

title: Nice Guy Johnny
synopsis: A master of the modern relationship dramedy, Edward Burns crafts a summery gem centered on baby-faced 24-year-old Johnny Rizzo, who’s about to trade his dream job in talk radio for some snoozeville gig that’ll pay enough to please his fiancée.

title: Nickel Boys
synopsis: Elwood Curtis, a young African-American boy, is sent to the Nickel Academy after he is falsely accused of a crime. While there, he meets a boy named Turner, and the two form a close friendship as they try to survive the horrors of the school and its corrupt administrators.

title: Never Sleep Again
synopsis: After attending a wedding on a steamboat traveling down the river Spree, three friends from Hamburg become stranded in a newly reunified Berlin. They begin a restless odyssey through the wastelands of a metropolis wavering between an unpredictable future and a lingering past.

title: Night Across the Street
synopsis: On the verge of a forced retirement, an elderly office worker begins to relive real and imagined memories from his life, from conversations with Long John Silver to cinema trips with Beethoven to a brief stay in a haunted hotel. All the while, a sinister end appears to be closing in…

title: Night and Fog
synopsis: The horrors of the Holocaust are shown in black and white, through documents, films and photographs found in German, Polish, and French archives. The footage of the now-empty camps, filmed only ten years after the liberation and shot in color, shows what remains of Auschwitz.

title: Night Games
synopsis: On the eve of his marriage to his fiancée, Jan returns to his childhood home—a sprawling estate stuffed with antiques—where he relives his memories of his beautiful, decadent, mercurial mother and finds himself forced to confront his unresolved Oedipal longings.

title: Night Moves
synopsis: Three radical environmental activists work together to execute the most intense protest of their lives: to blow up an Oregon hydroelectric dam—the very source and symbol of the resource-devouring industrial culture they despise. But their act of eco-terror plunges them into a moral maelstrom.

title: Night of the Kings
synopsis: When a young man named Roman is sent to an infamous lawless prison in the middle of the Ivorian forest, he is chosen to participate in a storytelling ritual. Roman begins to narrate the mystical life of a legendary outlaw to make his story last until dawn and give himself any chance of survival.

title: Night of the Living Dead
synopsis: When corpses emerge from a graveyard in search of fresh humans to devour, a disparate group of individuals takes refuge in an abandoned house. The pragmatic Ben does his best to control the situation, but when the murderous zombies surround the house, the other survivors begin to panic.

title: Night on Earth
synopsis: Five taxi drivers across the world have remarkable encounters over one eventful night. In LA, a talent agent decides her cabbie should star in her next movie. In Rome, a non-believing driver finds a dead bishop in his back seat. Meanwhile in Paris, a beguiling blind woman distracts her cabbie…

title: Night Tide
synopsis: A young sailor is attracted to a mysterious mermaid performing at a carnival, but soon suspects that the girl is actually a siren who draws men to their watery deaths during the full moon.

title: Nightcrawler
synopsis: A disturbed loner, living on the fringe of society, muscles his way into the lucrative underworld of tabloid crime journalism. Scouring the city for crimes of a shocking nature, he is aided by a veteran news director looking to raise station ratings.

title: Nightmare Alley
synopsis: Charismatic conman Stanton Carlisle endears himself to clairvoyant Zeena, learning the secrets of her mind-reading act and using it to grift the elite of 1940s New York society. With the virtuous Molly by his side, Stanton plots to con a dangerous tycoon with the aid of a mysterious psychologist.

title: Nights of Cabiria
synopsis: Rome, 1957. Irrepressible and fiercely independent sex worker Cabiria is robbed and left to drown by her boyfriend. When she is rescued, Cabiria resumes her life—and as she moves through the sea of Rome’s humanity, through adversity and heartbreak, Cabiria must rely on herself to stay standing.

title: Nimic
synopsis: The encounter of a professional cellist and family man with a stranger on the subway has unexpected and far-reaching consequences on his life.

title: Ninja Scroll
synopsis: A Journeyman ninja by name of Jubei stumbles upon a plague, an evil clan of demons, a national crisis, and a beautiful ninja girl.

title: Ninjababy
synopsis: Astronaut, forest-keeper, cartoonist. Young Rakel has a whole lot of other plans than becoming a mother. She would rather party, get drunk or stoned instead of sitting hours on the toilet. But she can’t ignore it. Is it her or the baby?

title: No
synopsis: In 1988, international pressure comes to bear on Chilean dictator Pinochet forcing him to call a referendum. The country will vote either yes or no on extending his rule for another eight years. Leaders of the “no” movement recruit Saavedra—a young advertising executive—to spearhead their campaign.

title: No Bears
synopsis: Director Jafar Panahi is relocated to a rural border town to remotely make a new film in nearby Turkey—the story of which comes to sharply mirror disturbing events that begin to occur around him. As he struggles to complete his film, Panahi finds himself thrust in the middle of a local scandal.

title: No Date, No Signature
synopsis: A seemingly minor traffic collision has far-reaching consequences as a well-meaning medical examiner becomes haunted by the death of a child he might have prevented. Soon, the examiner’s fate becomes inextricably bound up with that of the grieving family.

title: No Fear, No Die
synopsis: Dah from Benin and Jocelyn from the Caribbean make money through illegal cockfighting. But Pierre, the owner of the restaurant where the fights take place, pushes them to make the events more violent, and the disillusioned Jocelyn begins looking for a way out from the sordid dealings.

title: No Hard Feelings
synopsis: German-Iranian Parvis’s life revolves around pop culture, Grindr dates and raves. Through the refugee siblings Banafshe and Amon, Parvis rediscovers his past. A sensitive film about first love and the life as a migrant in Germany.

title: No Hard Feelings
synopsis: From Gene Stupnitsky, the co-writer and director of Good Boys, Jennifer Lawrence stars as Maddie, a young woman hired by the parents (Matthew Broderick and Laura Benanti) of a nerdy, awkward teenager preparing to go to college, for the purposes of socializing him.

title: We Shall Not Be Moved
synopsis: Socorro is a headstrong lawyer obsessed with finding the soldier who killed her brother during the 1968 student massacre in Tlatelolco, Mexico City. This need for justice masks an old guilt that has alienated her sister Esperanza and her son Jorge.

title: No Other Choice
synopsis: Looking for a job? When a happy family man is dismissed after twenty-five years of loyal service at a paper company, he finds the perfect solution to land his next role: truly eliminate the competition.

title: No Other Land
director: Basel Adra, Hamdan Ballal, Yuval Abraham, Rachel Szor
synopsis: Palestinian activist Basel Adra documents the destruction of his community in Masafer Yatta under Israeli occupation. He forms an unlikely alliance with Israeli journalist Yuval. For years, they fight against mass expulsion—forging a complex bond haunted by the extreme inequality between them.

title: No Sad Songs
synopsis: Canada, 1985. The LGBT+ community reacts to the AIDS crisis. Jim Black, 37, talks to the camera about his views on the premature death in store for him. Meanwhile, Catherine Hunt, who has a brother in the U.S. with the illness, discusses the values such a crisis brings out in families and friends.

title: Nobody Knows
synopsis: A twelve-year-old boy must care for his three younger siblings when their irresponsible mother leaves them with very little money in their Tokyo apartment When the money runs out, the children are forced to do whatever it takes to ensure they have food and hide the secret of their abandonment.

title: Nobody's Hero
synopsis: Clermont-Ferrand, central France. Médéric meets and falls in love with middle-aged sex worker Isadora, who is married. When the city center is the scene of a terrorist attack, Selim, a young, homeless guy, provokes a wave of paranoia by taking refuge in Médéric’s building.

title: Nocturama
synopsis: A group of adolescents prowl the streets and subways of Paris as they carry out a bombing plot. Afterward, they seek refuge in a shopping mall and soon lose themselves in consumer culture. Will they survive the encroaching authorities? Or, as the walls close in, will they even survive each other?

title: Nomadland
synopsis: Following the economic collapse of a company town in rural Nevada, Fern packs her van and sets off on the road exploring a life outside of conventional society as a modern-day nomad. As she journeys across the vast landscape of the American West, she meets people who become her mentors and comrades.

title: Non-Fiction
synopsis: Successful Parisian publisher Alain struggles to adapt to the digital revolution, and has doubts about the new manuscript of Léonard, one of his long-time authors—another autofiction recycling his affair with a minor celebrity. Selena, Alain’s wife and a famous actress, is of the opposite opinion.

title: Nope
synopsis: Two siblings running a horse ranch in California discover something wonderful and sinister in the skies above, while the owner of an adjacent theme park tries to profit from the mysterious, otherworldly phenomenon.

title: Normal
synopsis: Normal exposes the spectacle of gender in our everyday lives. Featuring scenes shot throughout Italy, the film observes individuals, from childhood to adulthood, navigating normative gendered behaviour. Normal reveals how the conditioning expectations of gender shape our smallest actions.

title: Nose Hair
synopsis: A man plucks a hair from his nostril. The hair wants back in.

title: Nosebleed
synopsis: Following a pivotal week for long-time best friends Lilah and Coby (whose friendship is morphing into something far more venomous and toxic), a flower that once delicately bloomed proves to just as easily draw blood with its thorns…

title: Nosferatu
synopsis: The mysterious Count Orlok summons Thomas Hutter to his remote Transylvanian castle in the mountains. The eerie Orlok seeks to buy a house near Hutter and his wife, Ellen. But Orlok is also the vampire Nosferatu, and when Hutter struggles to escape the castle, he knows that Ellen is in grave danger.

title: Nosferatu the Vampyre
synopsis: Sent to Transylvania to negotiate for a house purchased by the monstrous Count Dracula, Jonathan Harker leaves his idyllic town, turning a deaf ear to his wife Lucy’s ill omens. Soon, Jonathan finds himself trapped, and Dracula descends from the mountains towards Lucy, with death and plague in tow.

title: Nosso Diário
synopsis: The film presents behind-the-scenes footage of the film adaptation of Lavoura Arcaica (2001), which includes the creative process of the director and actors. Raquel Couto was Carvalho’s assistant director in the production.

title: Nostalghia
synopsis: The Russian poet Andrei Gorchakov travels through Italy accompanied by his guide and translator as he researches the life of an 18th Century Russian composer, Pavel Sosnovsky. There he meets Erland Josephson, a local pariah who declares that the world is coming to an end.

title: Not a Pretty Picture
synopsis: Mixing narrative and documentary filming in a unique way the story is autobiographical and is about a date rape dissecting the characters and circumstances around it.

title: Not All Is Vigil
synopsis: Antonio and Felisa have been together their whole and now that their health is more fragile their are afraid of not being able to take care of each other. The prospect of having to go into a retirement home appears on the horizon as a threat.

title: Not Junk Yet: The Art of Lary 7
synopsis: A film about New York musician, multi-instrumentalist, producer, sound artist, filmmaker and photographer Lary 7.

title: Notebook on Cities and Clothes
synopsis: Director Wim Wenders investigates the similarities between his craft, filmmaking, and that of the Japanese fashion designer Yohji Yamamoto—who, in the early 1980s, shocked and revolutionized the fashion world. Over a series of interviews in Paris and Tokyo, Yamamoto and Wenders spark a friendship.

title: Notes of a Crocodile
synopsis: News of a half-constructed building full of crocodiles brings a Chinese woman to Phnom Penh. She walks along the Mekong River, encountering humans and animals, in search of a lost friend.

title: Nothing Personal
synopsis: Anne leaves her previous life in Holland behind to lead a solitary, wandering existence in Ireland. During her travels, she discovers a house where hermit Martin lives. They close a deal: food in exchange of work. On one condition: no personal contact, no questions. Who will break the deal first?

title: Notice to Quit
synopsis: Andy Singer is a resilient yet struggling New York City rental agent caught amid his own eviction crisis. As his world begins crashing down around him, his estranged ten-year-old daughter, Anna, appears unannounced on his doorstep just as he’s being thrown out of his apartment.

title: Notorious
synopsis: The daughter of a German war criminal is engaged by an American agent to get close to one of her father’s Nazi associates. Her safety is compromised when suspicions arise, with potentially deadly consequences. As the two begin to fall for one another, the stakes become much higher.

title: Notturno
synopsis: Shot over three years on the borders between Iraq, Kurdistan, Syria and Lebanon, Notturno depicts the everyday struggles of people attempting to rebuild their lives amongst the devastating effects of civil wars, dictatorships, foreign invasions, and the deadly presence of ISIS.

title: We
synopsis: A kaleidoscopic portrait of people from the Parisian suburbs, their lives and work connected by the RER B commuter train that cuts through the city from north to south. A migrant mechanic, a care worker for the elderly, a writer, and the director herself, all make up the “we” of the title.

title: Nouvelle Vague
synopsis: Paris, 1959. A young Jean-Luc Godard is determined to make his debut film. Battling conventions, budgetary chaos and sceptical producers, he and his collaborators launch a quiet revolution in cinema, capturing the raw energy of the era.

title: Nowhere
synopsis: Follows a day in the lives of a group of Los Angeles high school students and the strange lives they lead.

title: Nude Caboose
synopsis: A jovial shirtless man attempts to rouse a contingent of lethargic dancers to life in Nude Caboose. His highjinks really get the deadbeats bustin’ out until something unusual catches his eye. Dance floor seduction distilled into a fateful moment.

title: Nude on the Moon
synopsis: Brilliant scientist Jeff inherits $3 million and builds his own rocketship. When he and the Professor reach the Moon, they find it inhabited by telepathic nudists with antennae. Suddenly, workaholic Jeff has time to learn about this thing called “love!”

title: Numéro zéro
synopsis: Jean Eustache films his 70-year-old grandmother Odette as she recounts her life—her childhood, her losses, her unfaithful husband, the children she lost, and the hardships she endured. She also expresses her concern for her grandson and great-grandson, whom she hopes to see grow up.

title: Nun or Never!
synopsis: The nuns living in a convent are happily melted together. When one nun digs a man up from the ground in the garden, she loses her grip on everyday life. Can secrets and harmony coexist?

title: O-Bi, O-Ba: The End of Civilization
synopsis: A year has passed since the nuclear war, and 800 sick and hungry people have taken shelter in a concrete building. They are waiting for the landing of The Ark, a mythical vessel that is supposed to bring collective salvation. But this news was invented by the ruling team…

title: O Fantasma
synopsis: Brooding, alienated Sergio works as a trash collector in Lisbon. At night, he roams the city streets seeking rough, anonymous sex with other men. One night Sergio becomes fascinated with the owner of a sleek motorcycle—a man who seems to be the embodiment of his tormented fantasies.

title: Our Father
synopsis: Three sisters are forced to live together during one of the most intense moments of the pandemic, in March 2021. From living together, discussions arise, a broken chicken on the kitchen floor and also an unusual idea, an unlikely plan and the possibility of saving the country.

title: Occidental
synopsis: In a boho Parisian hotel, two sexually and politically ambiguous Italians romp through a succession of blatantly artificial set pieces, stoking the prejudices of staff members and guests. Outside, riots rage and protesters march, threatening to spill into the feverish atmosphere gathering indoors.

title: Occupied
synopsis: In the West Bank, Palestine, young and disenfranchised artists discuss the choices they have made in pursuing their creative paths as a response to their surroundings. Amidst the persecution and marginalization they face, these artists celebrate life, love, and hope.

title: Occupied City
synopsis: Past and present collide in a modern-day Amsterdam haunted by its history. As a narrator details significant events that took place in the Dutch capital during the Nazi occupation from 1940 to 1945, contemporary footage shot between 2020 and 2022 captures the exact locations where they occurred.

title: Ocean Blues
synopsis: Isabel is an Actress. Xavier wants to be a Director. Isabel is looking for Love. Xavier is looking for the Love of his Life. Isabel is stuck on a bad relationship. Xavier is looking for one. Isabel sings while Xavier watches. Will love rule the Ocean? Or will the waves strike hard on both of them?

title: Oedipus Rex
synopsis: Amid the awe-inspiring landscapes of the Moroccan desert, the myth of Oedipus plays out, where the fated boy’s willful blindness to his nature unleashes a cataclysmic reckoning. Meanwhile, a prologue and epilogue set in twentieth-century Italy bring the legend of Oedipus into the modern day.

title: Of Fathers and Sons
synopsis: Syrian filmmaker Talal Derki returns to his homeland posing as a pro-jihadist photojournalist making a documentary on the rise of the caliphate. In a remote village, he meets radical Islamist leader and loving father Abu Osama, and the group of young boys—his son included—who idolize him.

title: Of Love One Lives
synopsis: “This research on tenderness, sensuality and love took me three years of life in Parma northern town of Italy where I contacted 7,000 people to have, at the end, 7 human beings that had really something to say about all this.” – Silvano Agosti.

title: Offside
synopsis: A group of football-obsessed young girls hatches an audacious plan to infiltrate the national stadium in Tehran—where women are banned from attending matches—to watch the World Cup qualifying game. Disguising themselves as men and dodging soldiers, the girls attempt to sneak into the stadium…

title: Oh, Canada
synopsis: Based on the novel “Foregone,” the plot focuses on draft evaders who fled the U.S. to Canada to avoid serving in Vietnam.

title: Oh, Hi!
synopsis: Iris and Isaac’s first romantic weekend getaway goes awry in a most unexpected way.

title: Oh, Moon!
synopsis: Eleven-year-old Yekta lives in a crumbling island mansion with her strict aunts and ailing grandfather, yearning for her absent mother. As dreams and surreal visions shape her solitude, she sets out in search of a mythical seagull with a child’s head.

title: Oh, Sun
synopsis: A young man arrives in Paris from Mauritania, where he hopes to make a ‘better’ life for himself. Finding it extremely hard to find a job or an apartment, although an educated man, and on the receiving end of condescending sexual advances from women, he will soon face discrimination from all angles.

title: Oh, Woe Is Me
synopsis: At a Swiss lakeside resort, a book publisher investigates a mysterious tale: the story of a god-like being entering the body of a man, Simon, to experience physical love with his wife, Rachel. Did this happen? Or is Rachel just covering up her infidelity to her husband?

title: OK, Good
synopsis: An aspiring Los Angeles actor spirals ever downward during the grueling process of trying to find steady work.

title: Old is the New
synopsis: Jessye, a young and modern Chinese woman, is sent to a small village in southern Italy to investigate the touristic potential of the region. Through the villagers, Jessye gradually learns more about the local traditions, now threatened by her presence and the tourism industry.

title: Old Joy
synopsis: Two old friends, Kurt and Mark, reunite for a weekend camping trip in the Cascade mountain range east of Portland, Oregon. When they arrive at their final destination, a hot spring deep in the forest, they must confront the divergent paths they have taken in life.

title: Oldboy
synopsis: Imprisoned by a sadistic captor for 15 years, Oh Dae-su survives only because of his will to get even with the villain who has made his life a living hell. But when he’s suddenly released without explanation, he embarks on a quest for revenge and finds himself plunged into a Kafkaesque conspiracy.

title: Olga's House of Shame
synopsis: The notorious exploitation entry that jumpstarted an entire “roughie” genre of S&M-tinged exploitation films. Audrey Cambell plays Olga, a sadistic jewel-thieving madame who hides in an abandoned mine and exacts all kinds of misery upon those who defy her—all told in oddball semi-documentary style.

title: Unfruitful Times
synopsis: After a tragic miscarriage, Nimet, a middle-aged woman, gets stuck in the conservative society in Istanbul while her beloved younger sister Öznur carries a burden of her own in the family, trying to struggle her own demons.

title: Omar
synopsis: A Palestinian baker regularly risks crossing the West Bank wall to see his beloved Nadia. Alongside his childhood friends Tarek and Amjad, he trains for armed resistance against Israeli soldiers. During one of these crossings, he is captured and subjected to violence and humiliation.

title: Omelia Contadina
synopsis: In Italy’s Alfina Plateau, hazelnut monoculture is destroying the local landscape. To pay homage to the farmers resisting change, JR and Alice Rohrwacher staged a funeral for peasant agriculture where workers carried enormous silhouettes of agriculturalists made by JR on their shoulders.

title: On Becoming a Guinea Fowl
synopsis: On an empty road in the middle of the night, Shula stumbles across the body of her uncle. As funeral proceedings begin around them, she and her cousins bring to light the buried secrets of their middle-class Zambian family.

title: On Body and Soul
synopsis: Two introverted people, both workers at an abattoir, find out by chance that they share the same dream every night. At first, they are puzzled and incredulous, but as they begin to accept this strange coincidence, they try to recreate in broad daylight what happens in their shared subconscious.

title: On Falling
synopsis: Aurora, a Portuguese migrant, works as a warehouse picker in Edinburgh. Trapped between the confines of a vast distribution centre and the solitude of her own bedroom, Aurora seeks out every opportunity to resist the alienation and isolation that threaten her sense of self.

title: On Fertile Lands
synopsis: Three friends decide to abandon their poverty-stricken village and try their luck in Çukurova, a cotton-growing region in southern Turkey.

title: On Football
synopsis: Sergio and his father haven’t seen each other for over 20 years. On the eve of the 2014 World Cup Sergio returns to his hometown, São Paulo. As days go by, their relationship starts to wander into unknown territory and their pact to watch the entire tournament together turns into a dangerous ritual.

title: On the Beach at Night Alone
synopsis: After an affair with a married man, celebrated actress Younghee decides to take some time out by traveling to Hamburg. During her walks through wintry parks and along riverbanks she wonders if his lover misses her as much as she does, and she attempts to become clear about her feelings and desires.

title: On the Road
synopsis: After his father dies, aspiring writer Sal Paradise meets Dean Moriarty, a charming ex-con married to the liberated and seductive Marylou. Sal and Dean bond instantly, and the three take to the road. Travelling across the country, they encounter a mix of people who impact their journey indelibly.

title: On the Seventh Day
synopsis: José, a young and hardworking bicycle delivery worker, is the captain of his soccer team in Sunset Park. When the team makes it to the finals, he and his teammates are thrilled. But his boss throws a wrench into the celebration when he tells José he has to work on Sunday, the day of the match.

title: On the Shore
synopsis: After their father’s death, four sisters reunite at the family inn they inherited. Yasemin, Nilüfer, Defne, and Deniz, each with their own paths in life, come together on the same shore after years apart and swim in the sea together.

title: On the Waterfront
synopsis: New Jersey. Under the sway of mob boss Johnny Friendly, who controls the labor union, Terry Malloy gets mixed up in the murder of dockworker Joey Doyle. When Terry falls for Joey’s sister Edie—who, along with Father Barry, rallies the workers to fight against corruption—Terry’s loyalties are tested.

title: The Monk and the Gun
synopsis: The Kingdom of Bhutan is to become a democracy and holds a mock election as a training exercise. In the town of Ura, an old lama orders a monk to get a gun to face the imminent change in the kingdom. Meanwhile, an American collector is in search of a valuable gun that falls in the lama’s hands.

title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
synopsis: In 1969 Los Angeles, TV star Rick Dalton and his longtime stunt double Cliff Booth make their way around an industry they hardly recognize anymore. Quentin Tarantino’s ninth film features a large ensemble cast and multiple storylines in a tribute to the final moments of Hollywood’s golden age.

title: Once Upon a Time in the Future: 2121
synopsis: In the future, the earth becomes uninhabitable due to climate crisis and famine. The few survivors from colonies and start to live in underground blocks. According to The Scarcity Laws of the Young Administration which controls system, old generations must be destroyed in exchange for new lives.

title: Once Upon a Time Yeşilçam - Abdurrahman Keskiner
synopsis: This is a documentary from Mehmet Güreli about the famous producer Abdurrahman Keskiner, who took great strides in promoting our country’s cinema to the world and who is one of the shining lights of our cinema.

title: Ek Tha Gaon
synopsis: In the Himalayan foothills, an 80-year-old woman and a 19-year-old girl are two of the seven remaining inhabitants of an abandoned village. The two women struggle with the choice to leave for an alienating city life or continue living in a lonely village.

title: Ondine
synopsis: A lyrical, modern fairy tale that tells the story of Syracuse (Colin Farrell), an Irish fisherman whose life is transformed when he catches a beautiful and mysterious woman (Alicja Bachleda) in his nets.

title: One Battle After Another
synopsis: Washed-up revolutionary Bob exists in a state of paranoia, surviving off-grid with his self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces after 16 years and she goes missing, the former radical scrambles to find her, father and daughter both battling the consequences of his past.

title: One Fine Morning
synopsis: In Paris, a young, widowed mother juggles her job as a translator with caring both for her young daughter and her elderly father, who suffers from a degenerative illness. Her life is further complicated when she embarks on a passionate affair with an old friend in an unhappy marriage.

title: One for the Road
synopsis: Although in self-denial about his drink problem, Mark seems to have easily mastered life between his demanding job as a construction manager and rambunctious forays into Berlin’s nightlife. That is, until he is stopped by the police one night.

title: One Life
synopsis: Follows British humanitarian Nicholas Winton, who helped save hundreds of children from the Nazis on the eve of World War II.

title: One Night at Babes
synopsis: At a rural Vermont dive bar called Babes, cribbage tournaments overlap afternoons of karaoke and nights of raucous queer dance parties. When the aging conservative townsfolk and the younger queer leftists begin sharing the same watering hole, a delicate allegiance flourishes.

title: One of Those Days When Hemme Dies
synopsis: Eyüp works relentlessly under the blazing sun during a tomato harvest in southeastern Turkey, driven by the urgent need to settle an impending debt. After a clash with his supervisor, he roams the city in search of a radical solution.

title: One Second
synopsis: During the Chinese Cultural Revolution, a convict escapes to seek out a film reel which contains footage of his estranged daughter. In a nearby village, he meets a homeless girl who is also searching for the film reel by any means. Soon, this reel becomes the root of their unexpected friendship.

title: One Shocking Moment
synopsis: No-budget auteur Ted V. Mikels (The Astro-Zombies, Blood Orgy of the She-Devils) ventures into lurid melodrama with a story of an executive and his nubile wife arriving in a new town and tangling with the S&M-tinged gang from the local exotic nightclub, leading to all manner of duos and triads.

title: One Sings, the Other Doesn't
synopsis: In 1960s Paris, aspiring singer Pomme befriends pregnant Suzanne. Pomme lends money for an abortion, but tragedy separates them. A decade later, they reconnect and vow to stay in touch via postcards, their lives transformed by the women’s liberation movement.

title: One to One: John & Yoko
synopsis: A moving look at the couple’s life upon their entry into a transformative 1970’s New York, exploring their musical, personal, artistic, social, and political world. At the core of the story are the One to One Concerts, John Lennon’s only full-length performances after The Beatles.

title: Only God Forgives
synopsis: Julian, a drug-smuggler thriving in Bangkok’s criminal underworld, sees his life get even more complicated when his mother arrives from the US. Thirsty for vengeance, she compels him to find and kill whoever is responsible for his brother’s recent death, who was killed after murdering a young woman.

title: Only Lovers Left Alive
synopsis: Centuries-old lovers Adam and Eve are vampires. He’s playing rock in Detroit while she’s hanging out in Tangiers. They struggle with depression and an ever-changing world and watch time go by, each finding stability in the other. However, the appearance of her brazen younger sister brings trouble.

title: Only the River Flows
synopsis: Dedicated police detective Ma Zhe is tasked with solving a series of brutal murders in a rural Chinese town. As pressure mounts from his superiors, a hasty arrest is made, but while his colleagues celebrate, several clues push Ma Zhe deeper into a desperate investigation of his own.

title: Onoda: 10,000 Nights in the Jungle
synopsis: Japan, 1944. Hiroo Onoda is sent to a small island in the Philippines where the Americans are about to land. His role will be to wage a guerilla war until the return of the Japanese troops. The Empire will surrender soon after, but for Onoda and his men, the war will go on for 10,000 more days.

title: Opium
synopsis: They say that the world got silent. They say that you went far away, with the cries, the laughs, the tomorrows. They say that the world has disappeared. But Oscar, he hears them whisper at night. Behind the walls, far from the men, at the end of the corridors…

title: Oppenheimer
synopsis: J. Robert Oppenheimer is appointed to work on the top-secret Manhattan Project. Oppenheimer and a team of scientists spend years developing the atomic bomb. Their work comes to fruition on July 16, 1945, as they witness the world’s first nuclear explosion, forever changing the course of history.

title: Or Something
synopsis: Two strangers show up at the same Brooklyn apartment to collect cash they’re both owed. Forced to spend the day together on a journey through New York City to find the promised money, the pair begin to open up to one another and reveal intimate details about their lives.

title: Oray
synopsis: Oray, a young Muslim, pronounces the Islamic term for separation, ‘talaq’, during an argument with his wife. The imam informs him of the consequences: Oray must now part from Burcu for three months.

title: Orchesterprobe
synopsis: In this early comedy short film from Germany, one of the musicians in a large group of instrumentalists extremely annoys the conductor during orchestral practice…

title: Orgy of the Dead
synopsis: A nudie cutie like no other, in which a pulp author and his wife are stranded in a graveyard and become slaves to the undead, enduring all manner of lascivious dancing. Starring Criswell (Plan 9 From Outer Space, whose Ed Wood wrote the screenplay here), filmed in gloriously garish candy colors.

title: Orlando
synopsis: Young aristocrat Orlando begins a quest for love and freedom in the court of Elizabeth I as a man. He is granted favours and property by the queen who commands the nobleman to never change. Orlando completes the search 400 years later as a woman, shaking off their biological and cultural destiny.

title: Orlando, My Political Biography
synopsis: In 1928, Virginia Woolf wrote Orlando, in which her protagonist changes sex in the middle of the story. A century later, writer and activist Paul B. Preciado sends a filmed letter to Virginia Woolf: Orlando has stepped out of fiction and is living a life she would never have imagined.

title: Orphan
synopsis: Hungary, 1957. A young Jewish boy, raised by his mother with idealized tales of his dead father, has his world turned upside down when a brutish man appears, claiming to be his true parent.

title: Orwell: 2+2=5
synopsis: The ultimate and comprehensive documentary film about the exceptional writer George Orwell.

title: The Dark Beast
synopsis: Three women are forced to flee their homes in a war-torn region of Colombia. Each woman’s journey is marked by terror and takes her on a trek from the depths of the jungle to the outskirts of Bogotá, where each must gather the strength needed to start a new life.

title: Oslo, August 31st
synopsis: Recovering drug addict Anders is given a day’s leave from his rehab center to apply for a job in the city. Over the course of one day and night, he tries to reconnect with his old friends and family in Oslo, where the ghosts of his past mistakes wrestle with the hope to see some future by morning.

title: Ostia
synopsis: Bandiera and Rabbino are two brothers who live in a seaside town near Rome, traumatized by their childhood memories of their abusive father. One day, a group of men find a woman lying unconscious and carry her to the brothers. The three of them embark upon a game exploring attraction and repulsion.

title: Other Cannibals
synopsis: Fausto, a bored middle-aged factory worker, connects with philosophy student Ivan online. They both share an extreme thought—that is dark and beyond human standards. In a remote valley eaten away by industrial towns, they meet and plan an escape from their everyday existence.

title: Outsider Girls
synopsis: Rafaela and Gabriela are young friends living in a fantasy world that is threatened when Rafaela discovers she is pregnant. She intends to have an abortion, but in Chile it’s illegal. They face the hostile system to guarantee a safe abortion for Rafaela, fighting to preserve their friendship.

title: Other People's Children
synopsis: When teacher Rachel falls in love with Ali, she also falls for his 4-year-old daughter Leila. Although she feels like a mother, Rachel cannot forget that Lelia is another woman’s daughter. Soon, she begins to long for her own child—but as a forty-something woman, she must make her decision swiftly.

title: Otherwise in Istanbul
synopsis: A performative documentary about dancer-choreographer Mihran Tomasyan; through a portrait of Tomasyan, the film also chronicles life in Istanbul in recent history through his personal archive.

title: Our Ark
synopsis: Humans are backing up the planet, creating 3D models of animals, rainforests, cities, and people—archiving as if ecological collapse could be staved off through a digital Noah’s Ark of beasts and objects. The artists explore our efforts to create a virtual replica of the real world.

title: Our Body
synopsis: Claire Simon observes the everyday operations of the gynecological ward in a public hospital in Paris. She meets patients in all stages of life—young and old, cisgender and trans. Unique stories of fertility, gender transitions, and cancer unfold, including the one of the filmmaker herself.

title: Our Grand Despair
synopsis: The peaceful cohabitation of bachelors Ender and Çetin is disrupted when they allow a friend’s sister to move in with them. The 30-something longtime friends are overwhelmed by the presence of the uncommunicative grieving Nihal, and soon they both fall for her.

title: Our Hospitality
synopsis: After twenty years away Willie McKay returns to his Appalachian homestead to take possession of his family home. On the trip, he falls for a young woman. The only problem is her family has vowed to kill every member of his family due to an ongoing feud that led to him being sent away as a baby.

title: Our Life
synopsis: Claudio is a young construction worker who lives happily with his pregnant wife Elena. One day at the construction site, he discovers the corpse of an illegal Romanian worker, but decides not to report the discovery for fear he’ll be out of a job. Claudio’s life suddenly takes an unexpected turn.

title: Our Little Sister
synopsis: Three sisters live together in Kamakura. When their father—absent for 15 years—dies, they travel to the countryside for his funeral and meet their teenage half-sister. Bonding quickly with the orphaned Suzu, they invite her to live with them and a new life of joyful discovery begins for them.

title: Our Time
synopsis: A family lives in the Mexican countryside raising fighting bulls. Esther is in charge of running the ranch, while her husband Juan raises and selects the beasts. When Esther becomes infatuated with a horse trainer named Phil, the couple struggles to stride through the emotional crisis.

title: Out
synopsis: A man suffers from a disturbed perception and is affected by hallucinatory images. Is it all reality or is he just dreaming? Ten seconds of life can flow like honey.

title: Out in the Dark
synopsis: Two young men—a Palestinian grad student and an Israeli lawyer—meet and fall in love amidst personal and political intrigue in this striking debut feature.

title: Outer Space
synopsis: A woman, terrorised by an invisible force, enters a house at night. The rasping of crickets and a distorted music give way to explosions, screams and garbled voices. In an eruption of panicked subjectivity, her face multiplies across the screen while flashes of solarised imagery invade the frame…

title: Outrage Coda
synopsis: Five years after the all-out war between the Sanno and Hanabishi crime families, former yakuza boss Otomo is working in South Korea for Mr. Chang, a go-between whose influence extends into Japan. But when tensions rise between Chang and the Hanabishi, Otomo returns to Japan to finally settle things.

title: Oxhide
synopsis: A cloud of anxiety hangs over a Chinese working-class family living in an impossibly cramped Beijing apartment, where they even share a bed. As the father’s leather-goods business slips toward bankruptcy, financial strain fuels tension with his wife and daughter.

title: Ozanlar ve Evler
synopsis: Part of the Old Houses, Old Masters documentary series about the Central Anatolia region, documenting the traditional architecture of the region and the masters who created it, together with its reflections in the poems of the bards who lived in mud-brick houses.

title: P. P. Rider
synopsis: Three teens embark on a wild road odyssey to rescue their kidnapped class bully from a gang of yakuza. En route, they run afoul of some cops, befriend a motley former gangster, and experience the first seismic shocks of growing up.

title: Pacific Club
synopsis: In 1979, the Pacific Club opened in a basement in La Défense—the business district of Paris. It was the first nightclub for young Arabs from the suburbs: a parallel world of dance, sweat, young lovers, and one-night romances. Azedine was one of them. He tells his generation’s forgotten story.

title: Pacifiction
synopsis: On the French Polynesian island of Tahiti, the High Commissioner of the Republic is a calculating man who coolly navigates both the “establishment” and the local underground.  He persists as rumors circulate about the sighting of a submarine whose presence could mean the return of nuclear testing.

title: Pain and Glory
synopsis: Salvador Mallo, a film director in his physical decline, reencounters or remembers his past: his childhood in a village, the first desire, his first adult love in 80s Madrid, the pain of that breakup. In recovering his past, Salvador finds the urgent need to recount it, and also finds his salvation.

title: Pale Flower
synopsis: In Tokyo’s criminal underworld, Muraki is released from prison after murdering a rival faction member, only to discover a new alliance has formed against a rising gang from Osaka. He meets the alluring Saeko at an illegal gambling event, developing a dangerous obsession with the enigmatic stranger.

title: Palindromes
synopsis: 12-year-old Aviva is determined to be a mother and does everything she can to achieve this. When her sensible mother intervenes, Aviva runs away from home. As a wanderer, she ends up in a strange world that is itself pregnant with chances, (maternal) insights, bizarre figures and experiences.

title: Palo Alto
synopsis: In the affluent Californian city of Palo Alto, three stories of teenage lust, boredom, and self-destruction intersect. April is torn between a flirtation with her coach and a crush on sweet stoner Teddy. Emily offers sexual favors to any boy—and Fred’s behavior may or may not be sociopathic.

title: Pan's Labyrinth
synopsis: Spain, 1944. Ofelia, a 10-year-old obsessed with fairy tales, moves with her pregnant mother to join her stepfather, a Fascist officer stationed in the mountains. Soon Ofelia discovers an overgrown labyrinth in the forest. There she meets Pan, an ancient satyr who claims to know her secret destiny.

title: Papicha
synopsis: Nedjma, an 18-year-old student passionate about fashion design refuses to let the tragic events of the Algerian Civil War keep her from experiencing a normal life. As the social climate becomes more conservative, she rejects the new bans set by the radicals and decides to put on a fashion show.

title: Papirosen
synopsis: Argentine director Gastón Solnicki focuses on four generations of his wildly volatile Jewish family haunted by a war rarely spoken of. Ten years of modern footage is folded into a huge archive of 8mm and VHS home videos, unveiling more than half a century of Solnicki’s singular family history.

title: Parade
synopsis: Juggling the roles of emcee and performer, Jacques Tati hosts a series of circus acts in a music hall for a family audience. The spectators—adults and children alike—are invited to participate in the rambunctious variety show, which encompasses acrobatics, magic tricks, and pantomime routines.

title: Paradise
synopsis: An Indian tourist couple arrive in the hill country of crisis-ridden Sri Lanka to celebrate their fifth wedding anniversary. But, when things take an unexpected turn, conflicts deepen revealing cracks in their relationship.

title: Parador Húngaro
synopsis: The story of a friendship spanning generations and boundaries: a Hungarian soldier escapes to the West in the ’50s. The son of a U.S. soldier runs to the East in the ’90s. Their paths cross in Bogotá, Colombia, and they become friends by sharing memories of the same land but of very different times.

title: Paragraph 175
synopsis: Historian Klaus Müller interviews survivors of the Nazi persecution of homosexuals because of the German Penal Code of 1871, Paragraph 175.

title: Paranoid Park
synopsis: An unsolved murder at Portland’s infamous Paranoid skateboard park brings detectives to a local high school. 16-year-old skater Alex decides to say nothing and gets into a moral odyssey where he must not only deal with the pain and guilt but also the consequences of his own actions.

title: Parasite
synopsis: Meet the Park Family, the picture of aspirational wealth. And the Kim Family, rich in street smarts but not much else. Masterminded by Ki-Woo, the Kim children scam their way as tutors into the wealthy household—and it’s not long before the Parks are obliviously bankrolling the whole Kim clan…

title: Paris Belongs to Us
synopsis: Young literature student Anne arrives in Paris for an entrance exam but is drawn into a theater troupe led by Gérard, who is staging Pericles. As she becomes entangled in their lives, the mysterious death of a friend and whispers of a hidden conspiracy cast a growing shadow over her world.

title: Paris by Night
synopsis: Police commander Simon Weiss, head of the division that supervises Paris’s demi-monde, starts out on his nightly tour of bars, discos and strip clubs, making sure once again that the owners don’t bend the rules too far.

title: Paris Is Burning
synopsis: New York City, 1980. In Harlem’s African American and Latinx drag-ball scene, rival houses compete in fierce contests for trophies. Meanwhile, legendary voguers, drag queens, and trans women—including Willi Ninja, Pepper LaBeija, and Venus Xtravaganza—reveal the rules and traditions of the scene.

title: Paris Memories
synopsis: Three months after a terrorist attack in a Paris bistro, Mia struggles to process her trauma and recall the events of that night. Returning to the city, she retraces her steps and connects with fellow survivors, hoping to find the stranger who comforted her.

title: Paris, Texas
synopsis: Travis walks out of the Texas desert after disappearing for four years. He is picked up by his brother Walt, who has been looking after Travis’ son Hunter. After slowly reconnecting with Hunter, Travis tries to bring his family back together by traveling to Houston in search of his estranged wife.

title: Parthenope
synopsis: Born in the Bay of Naples in 1950, Parthenope searches for happiness over the long summers of her youth, falling in love with her home city and its many memorable characters. But when tragedy strikes, and carefree romances lose their thrill, she decides to seek a deeper sense of purpose.

title: Party Girl
synopsis: Mary, a New York City nightclub scenester and social butterfly, rules the underground party scene. By day, however, she lacks purpose and struggles to make rent. When her aunt gets her a job at the local library, Mary initially waffles under the constraints—but soon begins to flourish.

title: Passages
synopsis: Lisl Ponger creates an imaginary map of the twentieth century on which the stories of emigration are engraved like well-worn tracks of occidental memory.

title: Passages
synopsis: Tomas and Martin are a gay couple living in Paris whose marriage is thrown into crisis when Tomas impulsively begins a passionate affair with young schoolteacher Agathe. But when Martin begins an affair of his own, Tomas must confront life decisions he may be unprepared—or unwilling—to deal with.

title: Passed by Censor
synopsis: As a prison letter examiner, Zakir’s job is to black out objectionable content from the inmates’ incoming and outgoing mail. In his free time, Zakir attends a writing course: his secret passion, and a means to socialise outside of work.

title: Passing
synopsis: A Black woman finds her world upended when her life becomes intertwined with a former childhood friend who’s passing as white.

title: Passing Summer
synopsis: A woman faces a variety of emotional crises as she spends the summer interacting in differing ways with friends, family, and lovers in this drama. Valerie is an author in her early thirties who has just moved to Berlin, while her best friend Sophie will be spending the summer in Rome.

title: Passion
synopsis: When Kaho and Tomoya announce their engagement at a celebratory dinner with friends, they are unprepared for the emotional turmoil unleashed once a past infidelity is revealed. Later, when Tomoya visits the apartment of his former classmate, further revelations of passion come to light.

title: Simple Passion
synopsis: Parisian professor and single mother Hélène falls into an addictive relationship with Russian diplomat Alexandre, with whom she has nothing in common. But as Alexandre continues to deny Hélène’s desire for a more meaningful relationship, her obsession with him begins to spiral out of control.

title: Past Lives
synopsis: Nora and Hae Sung are classmates at a school in Seoul. But their friendship ends when Nora’s family moves to Canada. Twelve years later, Nora notices that Hae Sung’s been searching for her on social media. They reconnect, but another dozen years pass before they meet during his visit to New York.

title: Paternal Leave
synopsis: Amid the harsh winter, 15-year-old Leo travels to Italy’s northern coast to find her long-absent father, Paolo. Her hope soon meets old wounds as Paolo’s settled life is upended. Through silence, tension, and unfamiliar words, father and daughter slowly edge toward connection.

title: Paterson
synopsis: Paterson is a bus driver in the city of Paterson, New Jersey. Every day, Paterson adheres to a simple routine: he drives his bus, observing the city and overhearing fragments of conversation; he writes poetry; he walks his dog; he goes to the same bar to drink a beer; he goes home to his wife Laura.

title: Patti Smith: Dream of Life
synopsis: Capturing both Patti Smith’s charged live performances and her informal interactions with friends and collaborators including Philip Glass, this documentary is also a touching tale of survival, detailing as it does the artist’s recovery from the unexpected deaths of her husband and her brother.

title: Pavements
synopsis: ‘90s indie-rock band Pavement reunites for their sold-out 2022 tour. But as preparations get underway, surreal tributes emerge: an off-Broadway musical adaptation of their songs, a museum devoted entirely to the band’s legacy, and a shamelessly awards-baiting Hollywood biopic.

title: Peacock
synopsis: Matthias is the head of a successful Rent-A-Friend agency: If you need company, want to improve your public appearance, or even manipulate someone–just rent a friend. Matthias plays every role his clients desire—like an actor—but in real life.

title: Pearl
synopsis: Trapped on her family’s isolated farm, Pearl must tend to her ailing father under the bitter and overbearing watch of her devout mother. Lusting for a glamorous life like she’s seen in the movies, Pearl’s ambitions, temptations, and repressions collide.

title: Peasants
synopsis: Between 1972 and 1975, peasants and workers grappled with the rise of coffee plantations and tenants’ demands. Their struggle highlights the cultural and political significance of land, contrasting Indigenous perspectives with the ruling class’s power dynamics.

title: Pebbles
synopsis: In the drought-stricken village of Arittapatti, southern India, an alcoholic father forces his young son to accompany him to retrieve his estranged wife. When the boy destroys the money for their return bus trip, they are left to endure a punishing 13km trek across the sunbaked landscape.

title: Peeping Tom
synopsis: Mark is a shy cameraman, but by night, he is a sadistic killer, stalking his victims with his camera to capture genuine, unadulterated fear. Mark becomes enamoured with Helen, who lives below him with her blind mother. But how long before he turns the deadly gaze of his camera towards her?

title: Penelope
synopsis: A mother of an autistic little girl, director Claire Doyon decides to try and find a way to make her child feel better by undertaking a long journey. Together they travel to the deepest corners of Mongolian Siberia, searching for a shaman with healing powers.

title: People on Sunday
director: Curt Siodmak, Robert Siodmak, Edgar G. Ulmer, Fred Zinnemann, Rochus Gliese
synopsis: Five young Berliners—including a taxi driver, a wine dealer, and a model—enjoy a typical Sunday outing to the countryside. Flirtations, rivalries, and other dynamics within the group begin to surface. But as the day ends, the prospect of returning to the weekday routine looms over the city-dwellers.

title: Pepe
director: Nelson Carlo de los Santos Arias
synopsis: A voice that claims to be the ghost of Pepe, the first and only hippopotamus ever killed in the Americas, tells his story. Beginning in Southern Africa and moving to South America, Pepe narrates his eventful life—from being owned by Colombian drug baron Pablo Escobar to his final days roaming free.

title: Pépé le Moko
synopsis: Pépé le Moko, one of France’s most wanted criminals, hides out in the Casbah section of Algiers. He knows the police will be waiting for him if he tries to leave the city. When Pépé meets Gaby, a Parisian girl, he is compelled to risk his life and leave its confines once and for all.

title: Pepi, Luci, Bom
synopsis: In 1980s countercultural Madrid, three women embark on wild misadventures. Pepi, a layabout, becomes an overnight success in the advertising world. Luci, a housewife, discovers her hidden masochistic side—while Bom is the lesbian punk who teaches Luci to find her pleasure in pain.

title: Perceval
synopsis: Filled with wonder at the sight of shining armor of a group of knights he has mistaken for angels, naive Perceval immediately solves the mystery of his destiny: he will become one of King Arthur’s knights. Valiant astride his horse, he rides from one ordeal to another, obsessed with a dream.

title: Perfect Blue
synopsis: Pop singer Mima Kirigow looks forward to a bright new career when she quits her chart-topping trio to become an actress.  When she lands a role in a sexually charged murder mystery, Mima’s life begins to fall apart.

title: Perfect Days
synopsis: Hirayama lives a life of blissful contentment, spending his days balancing his job as a caretaker of Tokyo’s public toilets with his passion for music, literature, and photography. His structured routine is slowly interrupted by unexpected encounters that force him to reconnect with his past.

title: Perfumed Nightmare
synopsis: Young Kidlat is a jeepney driver in the Philippines, but his dream is to leave his village and travel to Europe, the embodiment of the technological advancement he idolizes. Instead, he discovers inhumane modernity no better than what he left behind.

title: Permanent Vacation
synopsis: The young, alienated Aloysius Christopher Parker has no home, no school, and no job. He wanders an empty Manhattan searching for a sense of meaning, drifting in and out of strange encounters with eccentrics and vagabonds—always keeping just ahead of whatever it is that seems to be chasing him.

title: Persepolis
synopsis: In 1970s Iran, Marjane, an 8-year-old girl, dreams of saving the world. Cherished by her beloved family, she watches the events that led to the downfall of the Shah’s brutal regime through her young eyes. Now a punk-loving teenager, Marjane witness her country transform into an oppressive society.

title: Persona
synopsis: After Elisabet, a famed actress, lapses into silence from what appears to be a breakdown, she goes to a beach house with only Alma, a nurse, as company. Over the next weeks, as Alma struggles to reach her mute patient, they both find themselves experiencing a strange emotional convergence.

title: Personal Shopper
synopsis: Maureen, mid-20s, is a personal shopper for a media celebrity. The job pays for her stay in Paris, a city she refuses to leave until she makes contact with her twin brother who previously died there. Her life becomes more complicated when a mysterious person contacts her via text message.

title: Peter Hujar’s Day
synopsis: East Village, 1974. At his Second Avenue apartment, photographer Peter Hujar talks with his friend, the writer Linda Rosenkrantz. She tape-records Hujar as he recounts the previous 24 hours, detailing the ins and outs of his work and his interactions with personalities from New York’s art scene.

title: Peterloo
synopsis: Manchester, 1819. Workers, journalists, and intellectuals seek to cultivate class consciousness and demand better working and living conditions. When a crowd of 60,000 assembles in St. Peter’s Fields to demand reform, government-backed militias charge the protestors and violence erupts.

title: Petite Maman
synopsis: After the death of her beloved grandmother, eight-year-old Nelly meets a strangely familiar girl her own age in the woods. Instantly forming a connection with this mysterious new friend, Nelly embarks on a fantastical journey of discovery which helps her come to terms with this newfound loss.

title: Peter von Kant
synopsis: Film adaptation of cult play “The Bitter Tears.” Peter Von Kant, a successful, famous director, lives with his assistant Karl, whom he likes to mistreat and humiliate. Through the great actress Sidonie, he meets and falls in love with Amir, a young man of modest means.

title: Petrov's Flu
synopsis: With the city in the throes of a flu epidemic, the Petrov family struggles through yet another day in a country where the past is never past, the present is a booze-fueled, icy fever dream of violence and tenderness, and where – beneath layers of the ordinary – things turn out to be extraordinary.

title: Phantom Thread
synopsis: In 1950s post-war London, renowned dressmaker Reynolds Woodcock is at the center of British fashion, dressing royalty, movie stars, and socialites. But when Woodcock comes across Alma, a young, strong-willed woman—who soon becomes his muse—he finds his carefully tailored life disrupted by love.

title: Phantoms of July
synopsis: An East German waitress with a broken heart and an Iranian YouTuber with a broken arm seek solace from loneliness on a ghost hunt in the mountains.

title: Phases of Matter
synopsis: Doctors, workers, and inanimate residents of Istanbul’s state-run Cerrahpasa Hospital form a parallel world of their own.
