synopsis: When Tick accepts a drag performance job in Alice Springs, he sets off across the Australian outback with friends Bernadette and Adam in a flamboyant bus named Priscilla. Along the way, their bonds are tested as they face hostility, forge connections, and meet Bob, a mechanic who joins the ride.

title: The Adventures of Sukran the Lame
synopsis: A portrait of Şükran, whose life changed forever after a childhood accident at the age of ten. Longing for connection, she drifts through life, her every attempt to reach others met with quiet failure.

title: The African Desperate
synopsis: It’s MFA grad Palace Bryant’s final 24 hours in art school, and she is not going to the fucking graduation party! She needs to get back home to Chicago from Upstate New York, but that means surviving a hazy, hilarious, and hallucinatory odyssey, stumbling from academic critiques to backseat hookups.

title: The Age of Shadows
synopsis: Set in the late 1920s, this film follows the cat-and-mouse game that unfolds between a group of resistance fighters led by Gong’s character, trying to bring in explosives from Shanghai to destroy key Japanese facilities in Seoul, and Japanese agents trying to stop them.

title: The All Seeing Blind
synopsis: The golds of Saddam, the last booties of the Iraqi war, are smuggled into Turkey by hidden in the cotton. Blind workers are used to pick the gold from the cotton but the smugglers try to prevent any possible thefts by telling the workers that it is rock they are picking from the cotton.

title: The American Friend
synopsis: Jonathan Zimmerman, a picture framer who suffers from a fatal disease, crosses paths with Tom Ripley, who trafficks forged artworks. Drawn into an underworld of shady gangsters, Zimmerman is tempted to commit murder for a sum of money that would ensure the welfare of his family after his death.

title: The Amusement Park
synopsis: A senior gentleman goes for what he assumes will be an ordinary afternoon at the amusement park, only to find himself in a hellish nightmare instead. Thrown into a world where nobody cares about the elderly, the man must endure ruthless bikers, cynical fortune tellers, and deadly rollercoasters.

title: The Anabasis of May and Fusako Shigenobu, Masao Adachi and the 27 Years Without Images
synopsis: Artist Eric Baudelaire tracks the story of the Japanese Red Army through the ideological journey of two of its protagonists. Recounting this episode as the ‘anabasis’ of such radical faction, the Franco-American filmmaker captures a series of reflections that are both political and personal.

title: The Antenna
synopsis: The inhabitants of an apartment building are caught in a living nightmare when a radical, new communications technology goes horribly awry.

title: The Apartment
synopsis: Ambitious insurance clerk C.C. “Bud” Baxter attempts to climb the corporate ladder by loaning his apartment key to his superiors for their extramarital affairs. But the plan backfires when Bud unwittingly falls for the top executive’s current girlfriend, Fran Kubelik.

title: The Apparition
synopsis: Jacques, a journalist with a reputation as an impartial investigator, is recruited by the Vatican to determine the legitimacy of a saintly apparition in a small French village. Confronted with opposing views from all sides, Jacques gradually uncovers the truth and finds his belief system shaken.

title: The Apple
synopsis: In Iran, two twelve-year-old sisters have lived all their lives as prisoners of their impoverished father and blind mother. The father reasons that he is protecting his offspring from the world, but the arrival of a social worker sees an attempt made to persuade him that the girls need their freedom

title: The Apprentice
synopsis: A young Donald Trump, eager to make his name as a son of a wealthy family in 1970s New York, comes under the spell of Roy Cohn, the cutthroat attorney. Cohn sees in Trump the perfect protégé. Someone with raw ambition, a hunger for success, and a willingness to do whatever it takes to win.

title: The Ardor
synopsis: Mysterious and mystical Kai emerges from the Argentinean rainforest to rescue Vania, who has been kidnapped by mercenaries. Together, Kai and Vania face off against the men who have ruthlessly murdered Vania’s father, a poor farmer, and taken over his land.

title: The Aroma of Tea
synopsis: A small round object floats through an abstract space filled with inky shapes and narrow passages. The object must overcome all sorts of obstacles before it can make its way out of the labyrinth and into freedom.

title: The Assassin
synopsis: 9th century China. 10-year-old general’s daughter Nie Yinniang is abducted by a nun who initiates her into the martial arts, turning her into an exceptional assassin. One day, having failed in a task, she is sent to kill the man to whom she was promised, a cousin who leads a large military region.

title: The Assistant
synopsis: Jane, a college graduate and aspiring film producer, has landed her dream job as an assistant to an entertainment mogul. As she follows her daily routine, she grows aware of the abuse that colors every aspect of her workday, an accumulation of degradations against which she decides to take a stand.

title: The Aviator
synopsis: A biopic depicting the early years of legendary director and aviator Howard Hughes’ career, from the late 1920’s to the mid 1940’s.

title: The Awareness of Dreaming
synopsis: Veysel has never slept in his life. He sees this ability as a deficiency. Therefore, he hides this situation from everyone. He works at a luxury hotel to spend his free time at night. One night, one of the customers notices Veysel’s situation and a conversation begins between them.

title: The Babadook
synopsis: A single mother, plagued by the violent death of her husband, battles with her son’s fear of a monster lurking in the house, but soon discovers a sinister presence all around her.

title: The Baby of Mâcon
synopsis: When a beautiful male child is born in a medieval village savaged by pestilence and a plague of infertility, it is hailed as a fertility icon. However, the child’s ambitious 17-year old sister proclaims herself the mother, and she exploits the child’s powers, pitting herself against the Church.

title: Bad Lieutenant: Port of Call New Orleans
synopsis: After Hurricane Katrina, police sergeant Terence McDonagh rescues a prisoner, hurting his back in the process. He earns a promotion to lieutenant but develops an addiction to cocaine and painkillers. Six months later, Terence is assigned a case investigating the drug-related killing of a family.

title: The Balconettes
synopsis: As a heat wave brings a Marseille neighbourhood to the boil, three roommates gleefully meddle in the lives of their neighbours from their balcony. Until a late night drink turns into a bloody affair.

title: The Ballad of Genesis and Lady Jaye
synopsis: A major artistic figure of New York’s avant-garde and industrial music, Genesis Breyer P-Orridge and their wife and creative partner Lady Jaye, began a series of surgeries in 2000 to resemble one another. This bold, ambitious, and subversive act of love aimed to create a third ‘pandrogynous’ being.

title: The Ballad of the Wood
synopsis: Explores the traditional architecture of the houses of the Western Black Sea region for centuries has been passed down from father to son, from master to apprentice through secrets and a technique known as “canti”. Some of the houses shown have been standing for the last 100 to 150 years.

title: The Ballad of Wallis Island
synopsis: Eccentric lottery winner Charles dreams of reuniting his favorite musicians, Mortimer-McGwyer, by inviting them to perform a private show at his home on Wallis Island. When the bandmates – and former lovers – accept, old tensions resurface as Charles scrambles to save his dream concert.

title: The Barbarian Invasions
synopsis: Rémy, a Montreal college professor who is divorced and in his early fifties, learns that he is dying of liver cancer. During his final days, he is reunited with old friends, former lovers, his ex-wife, and his son, as they reflect on their exploits and political ideals.

title: The Baron of the Locks
synopsis: The lucky Baron wins a boat in a card game and takes off with his former lover to find new adventures. Adverse circumstances land them in a small town, where the Baron’s seafaring companion leaves for more attractive scenery offered by a wealthy local man.

title: The Basilisks
synopsis: In a small, poverty-stricken village in the South of Italy, Antonio, Francesco, and Sergio are three unemployed young men with uneventful lives. They while their time away roaming the streets and chasing girls, constantly hatching half-hearted plans but hopelessly trapped in their own lethargy.

title: The Battle of Algiers
synopsis: 1950s Algeria: Ali La Pointe is recruited to join the National Liberation Front, becoming involved in its armed insurgency against the French colonial powers. French paratrooper Colonel Mathieu is entrusted with squelching the uprisings. As violence escalates on each side, no act is too unthinkable.

title: The Batwoman
synopsis: A wacky scientist is kidnapping wrestlers and extracting their spinal fluid to create a race of Gill Men. So, two cops call in Batwoman to investigate. But when her mission leaves the scientist horrifically scarred, he seeks revenge. His plan? To capture Batwoman and turn her into Gill Woman!

title: The Beaches of Agnès
synopsis: Returning to the beaches that have been part of her life, Agnès Varda invents a kind of self-portrait-documentary. Agnès stages herself among excerpts of her films, images and reportages; weaving public and private struggles, loves and friendships, films and people, and the history of French cinema.

title: The Bear
synopsis: A bear cub is left alone, unable to feed or defend itself, when its mother is killed in a rockslide. Eventually, the cub attempts to bond with a male grizzly, which initially ignores it. When the grizzly is pursued by hunters, the cub comes to its aid and wins the grizzly’s affection.

title: The Beast
synopsis: In the near future where emotions have become a threat, Gabrielle finally decides to purify her DNA in a machine that will immerse her in her past lives and rid her of any strong feelings. She then meets Louis and feels a powerful connection, as if she had known him forever.

title: The Beast in the Jungle
synopsis: Over twenty-five years, in a huge nightclub, a man and woman wait for a mysterious event to take place. While they hold out in silence, everything around them is loud and in motion. From 1979 to 2004, from disco to techno, years pass as the duo wait and watch the world from the sidelines.

title: The Beasts
synopsis: An expatriate French couple operate an organic farm in the Spanish countryside. However, their earnest enthusiasm reeks of patronizing privilege to the handful of “hill people” families who have toiled on the land for generations, leading to tensions between locals and foreigners boiling over.

title: The Beat That My Heart Skipped
synopsis: Tom is 25 and seems to be following in the tracks of his father, a corrupted real estate agent. But an unexpected encounter brings him to think he could be the talented solo pianist he always wanted to be, as his mother was. He keeps working in real estate but starts preparing for an audition.

title: The Beekeeper
synopsis: In The Beekeeper, one man’s brutal campaign for vengeance takes on national stakes after he is revealed to be a former operative of a powerful and clandestine organization known as “Beekeepers”.

title: The Believer
synopsis: A young Jewish man develops a fiercely anti-Semitic philosophy. Based on the factual story of a K.K.K. member in the 1960s who was revealed to be Jewish by a New York Times reporter.

title: The Believer's Heaven
synopsis: The third and final evangelical cinema collaboration between Rev. Estus Pirkle and directors Ron and June Ormond depicts the glory of Heaven—with a clear warning for what awaits sinners. The rarest of the trilogy, never screened outside of the revival circuit.

title: The Big Fix
synopsis: On April 22, 2010 the Deepwater Horizon offshore drilling rig run by BP sunk into the Gulf of Mexico, creating the worst oil spill in history. Two documentarians set out to find the root cause of it all…

title: The Big Night
synopsis: The story of two very different half brothers, played by Benoît Poelvoorde and Albert Dupontel. The first is an old punk, who roams around with his dog and his belongings, while the second is the good boy full of future business plans, who goes wrong following his dismissal and divorce…

title: The Big Sick
synopsis: Pakistan-born comedian Kumail Nanjiani and grad student Emily Gardner fall in love but struggle as their cultures clash. When Emily contracts a mysterious illness, Kumail finds himself forced to face her feisty parents, his family’s expectations, and his true feelings.

title: The Bikeriders
synopsis: Chicago, 1960s. After a chance encounter at a local bar, strong-willed Kathy is drawn to Benny, the newest member of the Vandals, a motorcycle club. As the club evolves from a gathering place for outsiders into a more sinister gang, Benny must choose between Kathy and his loyalty to the club.

title: The Bird with the Crystal Plumage
synopsis: Sam, an American writer living in Rome, witnesses a brutal attack on a woman in an art gallery. As he grows obsessed with the incident, he is convinced that something he saw holds the key to identifying the maniac terrorizing the city. He launches his own investigation, heedless of the danger ahead.

title: The Bitter Tears of Petra von Kant
synopsis: Petra von Kant is a successful fashion designer—arrogant, caustic, and self-satisfied. She’s in a fairly satisfactory S&M relationship with her assistant, Marlene. But when she develops an obsession with fashion model Karin, things become far more complicated, and love intermingles with hate.

title: The Blazing Sun
synopsis: Ahmed, a young agricultural engineer, helps the farmers produce a superior sugar cane crop. Taher Pasha, a wealthy land owner, feels threatened by his recent production prosperity. Ahmed is in love with Pasha’s daughter, but due to their fathers’ rivalry, he is compelled to hide their relationship.

title: The Bling Ring
synopsis: In the fame-obsessed world of Los Angeles, a group of teenagers use the internet to track their favorite celebrities’ whereabouts in order to rob their homes. Based on true events, the group stole more than $3 million in luxury goods from the homes of stars like Paris Hilton and Orlando Bloom.

title: The Bloodiest
synopsis: In a futuristic Cameroon, Majolie and Chouchou are sex workers frequented by the political class—a corrupt and sex-obsessed crop of powerful men. When one of their influential clients dies under compromising circumstances, Majolie and Chouchou do all they can to dispose of the body.

title: The Bloody Child
synopsis: A young US Marine, recently back from serving in the Gulf War, is found digging a hole in the middle of the Mojave Desert while his car, with his wife’s bloody and lifeless body inside, is found nearby. A female officer begins probing the inexplicable death.

title: The Blue Angel
synopsis: Respected schoolteacher Rath learns of his pupils’ infatuation with postcards depicting a nightclub songstress. To investigate the source of indecency, Rath goes to the Blue Angel nightclub and is fatefully seduced by the smoldering Lola-Lola, triggering the downward spiral of his life and fortune.

title: The Blue Door
synopsis: Antoine returns to his motherland after a very long time to attend his mother’s funeral. His daughter Marie-Ange calls him and asks for a story. He starts telling her a tale he was told as a child…

title: The Blue Room
synopsis: A man and a woman, secretly in love, alone in a room. They desire each other, and even bite each other. In the afterglow, they share a few sweet nothings. At least the man seemed to believe they were nothing. Now under investigation by the police and the courts, what is he accused of?

title: The Blue Star
synopsis: It all happened on a dark night. The Man was tired of being a stranger, tired of being insulted by others simply because he speaks Arabic to a wife who speaks French with their beloved mixed race son. He looked at the sky and shouted. But that night, far in the cosmos, a voice answered.

title: The Body
synopsis: A detective searches for a body which has gone missing from the morgue.

title: The Bohemian Life
synopsis: In Paris, three penniless artists, all kindred spirits, share their views of the world and their love stories over a bottle of wine. Marcel is an unsuccessful writer with a penchant for monumental drama, Rodolfo, an Albanian painter with no visa, and Schaunard, an avant-garde composer of noise.

title: The Bones
synopsis: The world’s first stop-motion animation film is excavated as Chile drafts a new Constitution. The film shows a girl performing a ritual exorcism to free Chile from its feudal heritage. Among a dance of bones, organs, and body parts, the spirits of two damned Secretaries of State are invoked.

title: The Booth
synopsis: A female frisking booth inside a crowded shopping mall stands as a silent ally to a forbidden romance.

title: The Boss of It All
synopsis: When Ravn started his IT firm, he invented a nonexistent CEO to hide behind when unpopular decisions needed making. However when he wants to sell the company, Ravn must get this boss to appear and sign away the firm. So he hires unemployed actor Stoffer to play what becomes the role of a lifetime.

title: The Box
synopsis: Teenager Hatzín travels from Mexico City to collect the remains of his father, found in a communal grave in Northern Mexico. But a casual encounter with a man who he shares a physical resemblance with introduces both doubt and hope around the truth.

title: The Boxer from Shantung
synopsis: Early 1900s. A young Northern Chinese man’s fearsome boxing ability allows him to muscle his way to the top of the Shanghai underworld. But when he refuses to become an assassin for the city’s leading gang, he finds life on Shanghai’s mean streets far riskier than the boxing ring.

title: The Burning Hell
synopsis: Never intended for general exhibition, this evangelical outreach film was made to depict, in the most vivid cinematic terms, where non-believers and sinners shall go lest they be saved. Based on the sermons of a Southern Baptist preacher and directed by notorious exploitation filmmaker Ron Ormond.

title: The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari
synopsis: At a carnival, Francis and his friend Alan encounter the eccentric Dr. Caligari. He presents them the hypnotized Cesare, who can allegedly predict the future. Shockingly, he predicts Alan’s death, and by morning his prophecy has come true. Thus, Cesare and Dr. Caligari become the prime suspects.

title: The Canal
synopsis: Based on real events, this story traces a young district governor’s moral awakening as he confronts a powerful landowner in rural Adana. What begins as a routine bureaucratic act soon turns into a struggle to shield villagers from exploitation, greed, and the threat of malaria.

title: The Canyons
synopsis: Modern-day Hollywood. After growing bored with her scheming movie producer boyfriend, Christian, young actress Tara rekindles an affair with a former flame. When Christian learns of her infidelity, the couple is plunged into the darkest depths of human desire.

title: The Capsule
synopsis: A group of seven stone-faced women are awakened by candlelight in a palatial mansion, perched at the top of a Cycladic rock. They engage in a bizarre series of ritualistic endeavours for their matriarch, which could prove to be just as incredible as life itself, before receding back to their lair.

title: The Card Counter
synopsis: William Tell is an ex-military interrogator living under the radar as a low-stakes gambler. When he encounters a young man looking to commit revenge against a mutual enemy, he takes him on the casino circuit to set him on a new path. However, the ghosts of the past will not release him so easily.

title: The Cathedral
synopsis: From the ‘80s to the 2000s, the quiet rise and fall of a Long Island family is seen through the eyes of its youngest member, Jesse. The family’s struggle to remain middle-class is observed in fragments, filtered through Jesse’s visceral memories of his youth.

title: The Celluloid Closet
synopsis: The history of LGBTQ+ representation in Hollywood is traced from early coded portrayals and damaging stereotypes to more open and nuanced depictions. Narrator Lily Tomlin reflects on cinema’s role in shaping—and distorting—queer visibility.

title: The Cemil Show
synopsis: Long-suffering mall cop Cemil auditions for the bad guy role in a remake of a Turkish B-movie. Even with help from the original bad guy, he doesn’t have a chance. He keeps pushing, until he begins to lose his grip.

title: The Challenge
synopsis: A falconer enters an important tournament in Qatar, competing with wealthy Qatari sheikhs with a passion for amateur falconry. With their opulence on full display, the men fly their prized birds on private jets, race SUVs through the dunes, and take their pet cheetahs for rides in their Ferraris.

title: The Chambermaid
synopsis: Eve works long hours as a maid at a luxurious hotel in Mexico City. A single mother, Eve hopes that her diligence will get her a coveted spot on an executive floor. She enrolls in the hotel’s adult education program, but soon discovers that it’s not necessarily the most hard-working who get noticed.

title: The Chaser
synopsis: Joong-ho is a dirty detective turned pimp in financial trouble as several of his girls have recently disappeared without clearing their debts. While trying to track them down, he finds a clue that the vanished girls were all called up by a same client whom one of his girls is meeting with right now.

title: The Childhood of a Leader
synopsis: In the aftermath of World War I, 7-year-old Prescott moves to a château in France with his German mother and his American diplomat father. As he witnesses his father’s part in the 1919 Paris Peace Conference, the boy learns to manipulate the adults around him, developing a terrifying ego.

title: The Children Act
synopsis: Fiona Maye is an eminent high court judge presiding over ethically complex cases. As the demands of her job cause her marriage to Jack to reach a tipping point, Fiona is asked to rule on the case of Adam, a young boy who is refusing a life-saving blood transfusion on religious grounds.

title: The Choice
synopsis: Poverty and misery are rife in Gourga, a village in the Sahel. The inhabitants must choose: stay and await international assistance or leave for more fertile regions in the country. Salam, a peasant, and his family opt for the second solution, with all the sacrifices this entails.

title: The Christophers
synopsis: The estranged children of a once-famous artist hire a forger to complete his unfinished works so they can be discovered and sold after his death.

title: The Chronology of Water
synopsis: A young woman finds her voice through the written word and her salvation as a swimmer—ultimately becoming a triumphant teacher, mother and a singular modern writer.

title: The Circle
synopsis: Over the course of a single day in Tehran, a chain of loosely connected stories follows several women as they move through hospitals, streets, police stations, and private homes. Their paths intersect as they confront restrictions surrounding birth, work, and relationships.

title: The City of Lost Children
synopsis: Krank is a deranged scientist who kidnaps children and steals their dreams in an attempt to reverse his aging process. When Krank’s henchmen kidnap his brother, fisherman and former circus strongman One sets out on a journey to Krank’s laboratory, accompanied by a little orphan girl called Miette.

title: The Class
synopsis: François and his fellow teachers prepare for a new year at a high school in a tough neighborhood of Paris. Neither stuffy nor severe, François’ frankness often takes the students by surprise. But his classroom ethics are put to the test when his students begin to challenge his methods.

title: The Climb
synopsis: Kyle and Mike are best friends who share a close bond — until Mike sleeps with Kyle’s fiancée. A stormy but enduring relationship ensues between Kyle, with limitless patience, and Mike, who only lives his life by sowing discord in that of others, across many years of laughter, heartbreak, and rage.

title: The Clowns
synopsis: A young boy watches a circus get constructed outside his bedroom window—thus begins Federico Fellini’s “docu-comedy”, a personal, genre-bending ode to one of his lifelong obsessions.

title: The Coast Guard
synopsis: A group of soldiers on South Korea’s border with the North become at odds with the local villagers and themselves.

title: The Code
synopsis: Early-pandemic, Jay and Celine head to a rental house to reconnect. While Celine films a documentary on their disintegrating relationship, a recently ‘cancelled’ Jay becomes increasingly paranoid about his portrayal, setting up hidden cameras to spy on Celine.

title: The Color of Pomegranates
synopsis: In 18th-century Armenia, the life of poet and musician Sayat Nova unfolds through his verses and a series of stylized tableaux. His journey follows him from humble weaver to royal minstrel and later to monastic life, concluding with the invasion of the Persians.

title: The Color Wheel
synopsis: JR, an aspiring news-anchor, forces her younger brother Colin to embark on a road trip to move her belongings out of her professor-turned-lover’s place. Traveling through New England, they uncomfortably run into old school-mates or revisit familial history from which they have long since diverged.

title: The Comb
synopsis: The film opens in the shadowy bedroom of a sleeping beauty and seems to enter her mind and burrow into her dreams. Based on the text by the Austrian writer Robert Walser, The Comb is an exploration of the subconscious visualized as a labyrinthine playhouse haunted by a doll-like explorer.

title: The Compass Is Carried by the Dead Man
synopsis: Chencho is a boy who wants to cross the border to get to Chicago to be with his older brother. On the way, he meets an old man who offers him a ride in his wagon. But the man dies. Chencho continues on his journey with the dead man sitting by his side.

title: The Conformist
synopsis: Set in 1930s Italy, Marcello has spent his life accommodating others, in search for a sense of belonging. When he is drawn into the fascist party, he agrees to assassinate a political refugee who turns out to be his former college professor.

title: The Consequences of Love
synopsis: Titta Di Girolamo, a 50-year-old Italian man, lives in an anonymous hotel room in Switzerland. His time is spent smoking and observing the hotel’s patrons, constantly waiting for something to happen. What is his dark secret, and what is the story of the mysterious suitcases delivered to his door?

title: The Conversation
synopsis: Surveillance expert Harry Caul is hired to tail a young couple. Tracking the pair through San Francisco’s Union Square, the man manages to record a cryptic conversation between them. Tormented by memories of a previous case that ended badly, Caul sets out to determine if the couple are in danger.

title: Kaathal
synopsis: The captivating narrative of Mathew & Omana, a seemingly harmonious couple residing in a serene village, Teekoy, Kottayam,  Kerala. Mathew Devassy, a retired Co-operative bank secretary with a stellar reputation in the community, reluctantly steps into the realm of politics to contest a by-election.

title: The Corridor
synopsis: During a male-bonding weekend in the country, five friends hung up on the downfalls of reaching middle age discover a spectral corridor in the woods, and what should have been a relaxing weekend getaway ends up turning into a dangerous battle from which no one will emerge unscathed.

title: The Cow
synopsis: In a small village in Iran, Hassan cherishes his cow more than anything in the world. While he is away, the cow mysteriously dies, and the villagers protectively try to convince Hassan the cow has wandered off. Grief stricken, Hassan begins to believe he is his own beloved bovine.

title: The Criminals
synopsis: As night falls in a small town in Anatolia, Emre and Nazlı, a young couple, look for a room to make love. They are rejected from the hotels because they do not have a marriage certificate. When they think they have found a trick, the situation quickly gets out of hand.

title: The Crowd
synopsis: Inspired by İlhan Mimaroğlu and Freddie Hubbard’s experimental track, The Crowd is a surreal animated game of associations, blending anti-war poetry, jazz, and musique concrète into a visual fantasia.

title: The Currents
synopsis: During a business trip abroad, Lina, a fashion designer from Argentina, jumps off a bridge into the freezing river water in a mechanical gesture. Back in Buenos Aires, she decides not to tell anyone, but develops a physical fear of water that prevents her from carrying out everyday activities.

title: The Cut
synopsis: One night in 1915, the Turkish police round up all the Armenian men in the city, including Nazaret, who is separated from his family. Years later, after surviving the horrors of the genocide, he hears that his twin daughters may also still be alive, and travels the globe in search of them.

title: The Death of Mr. Lăzărescu
synopsis: After suffering terrible headaches and stomach cramps, Mr. Lăzărescu, a lonely 63 year-old man, calls for an ambulance, beginning one man’s hellish journey through Bucharest hospitals in search of proper medical care. As the night unfolds, his health starts to deteriorate fast.

title: The Death of Stalin
synopsis: Moscow, 1953: when tyrannical dictator Joseph Stalin drops dead, his parasitic cronies enter a frantic power struggle to be the next Soviet leader. The dweeby Georgy Malenkov, the wily Nikita Khrushchev, and the sadistic secret police chief Lavrentiy Beria bumble and backstab their way to the top.

title: The Deep Blue Sea
synopsis: Hester Collyer is the wife of an upper-class judge and a free spirit trapped in a passionless marriage. Her encounter with Freddie Page, a young, troubled former royal Air Force pilot, throws her life in turmoil, as their erotic relationship leaves her emotionally stranded and physically isolated.

title: The Deer Hunter
synopsis: In 1968, three lifelong friends from a working-class Pennsylvania steel town prepare to ship out to Vietnam to fight in the war after one last hunting trip. There, their dreams of military honor are quickly shattered by the inhumanities of war—and those who survive are haunted by their memories.

title: The Delta
synopsis: At a Memphis movie house, romance sparks between Lincoln, an affluent white teenager, and Minh, the migrant son of a G.I. and a Vietnamese woman. But when they take a spontaneous trip down the Mississippi in Lincoln’s father’s boat, the two lovers soon uncover what they do not have in common.

title: The Descent
synopsis: A year after severe emotional trauma, Sarah spends some time exploring caves with her friends. After descending underground, the women find evidence of an earlier expedition and learn they are not alone. Underground predators inhabit the crevasses, and they have a taste for human flesh.

title: The Desert of the Tartars
synopsis: Drogo, a young lieutenant, is assigned to the defense of an isolated fortress in a mountainous desert region. The garrison’s mission is to fight off any attacks by the Tartars. In reality though, the real enemy is time, sapping their lives and vitality as they wait for an enemy that never shows up.

title: The Diary of an Exile
synopsis: The film unfolds around the memories and daily life of revolutionary musician Fuat Saka, who had to leave Türkiye just before the military coup d’etat in 1980 because of his political views and a fascist attack against him on the eve of 1979.

title: The Disappearance of Shere Hite
synopsis: Shere Hite’s bestselling book, The Hite Report, liberated the female orgasm by revealing private experiences of thousands of anonymous survey respondents. Her findings rocked the American establishment and presaged conversations about sexuality, and bodily autonomy. So how did she disappear?

title: The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie
synopsis: Six upper-class people sit down to a dinner that is continually delayed, their attempts to eat thwarted by events both real and imagined, from terrorist attacks to ghostly apparitions. In their quest for a lavish feast, the line between reality and dreams becomes unclear, and all hell breaks loose.

title: The Doll
synopsis: When Fatma Teyze moves into the rundown mansion after her house is demolished due to urban renewal, she meets Dunya, an Afghan refugee who has just given birth. The two realize that there are beings other than themselves in the house.

title: The Doom Generation
synopsis: Amy Blue (Rose McGowan) is wallowing in a boring relationship with her high school sweetheart Jordan White (James Duval) when white-hot drifter Xavier Red (Johnathon Schaech) literally lands on their car and climbs aboard.

title: The Double Life of Véronique
synopsis: Polish choir soprano, Weronika, has a double. She is Véronique, a French music teacher. They have no blood relation and lead very different lives but are otherwise identical: sharing a sublime gift for singing, a cardiac disorder, and a mystical awareness of each other’s existence.

title: The Drama
synopsis: Charlie and Emma are engaged to be wed. But just one week away from the ceremony, the happy couple share their most intimate secrets at dinner with friends—and their relationship is put to the test by one shock revelation.

title: The Drawing Master
synopsis: Paul Young, a teacher of drawing and perspective at the Ontario College of Art of Design, goes from his final classes as a teacher into his first year of retirement. After 33 years of dedication to his students, he strives to become the artist he always dreamed of being.

title: The Dream of Lu
synopsis: After a suicide attempt, Lucía needs to find new meaning in her life in order to move on and face the loss of her son. She assists a therapy group for parents, finding small miracles and new ways of healing on an unexpected journey that will lead her to rediscover her place among the living.

title: The Dreamers
synopsis: Paris, ‘68. American student Matthew meets twins Theo and Isabelle at a protest. Sharing a love for film, the three become fast friends and spend summer at the twins’ bohemian flat. As riots rage outside, the trio embarks on a sensual and transgressive journey that will alter Matthew’s life forever.

title: The Driller Killer
synopsis: Reno is a struggling artist pushed to the edge by the economic realities of New York living in the late seventies and the punk band practicing in the apartment below. His grip on reality soon begins to slip, and he takes to stalking the streets with his power tool in search of prey.

title: The Driver
synopsis: An enigmatic man of fast cars and few words, the Driver excels at manoeuvring getaway cars, making him quite in demand in criminal circles. His notoriety infuriates the Detective, who becomes obsessed with taking him down. Luckily the Player, a resourceful woman, helps him elude the Detective.

title: The Duke
synopsis: In 1961, Kempton Bunton, a 60-year old taxi driver, stole Goya’s portrait of the Duke of Wellington from London’s National Gallery. His ransom notes stated that he would return the painting on condition that television should be free to the elderly. What happened next became the stuff of legend.

title: The Duke of Burgundy
synopsis: Day in and day out, lovers Cynthia and Evelyn enact an elaborate sadomasochistic fantasy as mistress and maid. But as their ritual of domination and submission begins to turn stale, Cynthia yearns for something more conventional, while Evelyn tries to push their taboos even further.

title: Ek Ghar
synopsis: Rajanna and Geeta arrive in the city with the hope of building a cozy little home. When they find their dream house, their happiness knows no bounds. Things are fine till, one day, a loud workshop opens up next door.

title: The Echo
synopsis: In a remote and solitary valley, punished by winter and drought, the children learn to gaze upon death, illness and love. A story about feeling vertigo in the face of life, about growing up.

title: The Edge of Heaven
synopsis: The lives of six German-Turkish immigrants are drawn together by circumstance: An old man and a sex worker forging a partnership, a young scholar reconciling his past, two young women falling in love, and a mother putting the shattered pieces of her life back together.

title: The Edukators
synopsis: Activists Jan, Peter, and Jule lead a silent revolution as the self-styled “Edukators”. Breaking into houses by the cover of the night, they seek to unnerve the wealthy through their protest art—until a terrible incident threatens not only their political beliefs but their trust in each other.

title: The Eggregores' Theory
synopsis: He cannot remember much from that time. Things have faded, like the colors of a painting exposed to too much light. The only thing bothering him is the music. It keeps popping up in his mind. He thinks he’s forgotten what it meant. He cannot get it out of his head.

title: The Element of Crime
synopsis: Exiled in Cairo, ex-cop Fisher relives his memories of his last case when working in dystopian Europe, where his disgraced former mentor asks him to solve a series of murders involving lottery ticket sellers. Using his mentor’s controversial methods, Fisher retraces the steps of a suspect.

title: The Elephant Man
synopsis: In Victorian London, Dr. Treves discovers Joseph Merrick in a circus side show. Born with a congenital disorder, Merrick uses his disfigurement to earn a living as the “Elephant Man.” Treves takes an interest in Merrick’s welfare, discovering that his rough exterior hides a refined soul.

title: The Emissaries
synopsis: One of the jesters of a theatre company has to leave after the upcoming performance. The performance itself will determine which one that is going to be.

title: The Empire
synopsis: In a quiet French fishing village, a special baby is born—a child so unique and peculiar that it unleashes a secret war between One and Zero, two opposing forces from the depths of outer space. But as they await a final battle, the imperial legions struggle to win humanity over to their causes.

title: The Encampments
synopsis: Students flooded Columbia University’s lawn to create the Gaza Solidarity Encampment in order to pressure their university to divest from the US and Israeli weapons companies.

title: The End
synopsis: Twenty-five years after the Earth’s environmental collapse, Mother, Father and Son cling to a sense of normalcy while confined to a palatial bunker. But when a Girl turns up at their doorstep, with her own past and perspective, the family’s blind optimism begins to unravel.

title: The End of Poverty?
synopsis: Global poverty did not just happen. It began with military conquest, slavery and colonization that resulted in the seizure of land, minerals and forced labor. Today, the problem persists because of unfair debt, trade and tax policies — in other words, wealthy countries taking advantage of poor.

title: The End of Summer
synopsis: Manbei Kohayagawa runs a brewery on the verge of bankruptcy. Lately, the old man finds comfort with his former mistress, to the dismay of his three daughters—Akika, a widow and a mother; Fumiko, whose husband tries to save the brewery; and Noriko, who refuses to marry the man chosen by his family.

title: The End of the Tour
synopsis: In 1996, Rolling Stone reporter David Lipsky begins a five-day interview with acclaimed novelist David Foster Wallace following the publication of his groundbreaking epic, Infinite Jest. As the days progress, a tenuous yet intense relationship develops between the journalist and his subject.

title: The End We Start From
synopsis: As London is submerged below floodwaters, a woman gives birth to her first child. Days later, she and her baby are forced to leave their home in search of safety. They head north through a newly dangerous country seeking refuge from place to place.

title: The Enigma of Kaspar Hauser
synopsis: Kaspar Hauser lives locked in a cellar, where he cannot see or speak to anyone. One day, a mysterious man pulls him out, teaches him to walk and talk, and then leaves him in the middle of a town square with a letter in his hand addressed to the authorities. Kaspar’s journey begins…

title: The Equalizer 3
synopsis: Robert McCall finds himself at home in Southern Italy but he discovers his friends are under the control of local crime bosses. As events turn deadly, McCall knows what he has to do: become his friends’ protector by taking on the mafia.

title: The Eternal Daughter
synopsis: Julie, a filmmaker out of inspiration, retreats with her elderly mother to a secluded manor in the English countryside — a place her mother once knew in her youth. Julie is soon unsettled by the strange atmosphere and a growing sense that an unspeakable secret haunts the house.

title: The Eternal Memory
synopsis: Augusto and Paulina have been together for 25 years. Eight years ago, he was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease. Both fear the day he no longer recognizes her.

title: The Executioner
synopsis: In Franco-era Spain, José Luis cannot find a girlfriend because he works in a funeral parlor. Meanwhile, Carmen cannot find a boyfriend because her father is an executioner. But after the two lost souls meet and marry, José Luis is coaxed into taking over his father-in-law’s grim profession.

title: The Exit 8
synopsis: A man gets lost in an underground passage. He follows the “guide” through the passage, but one after another, strange things happen to him. Is this space real? Or an illusion? Will the man be able to escape the passage?

title: The Eyes of Orson Welles
synopsis: Granted unprecedented access to hundreds of sketches, drawings, and paintings by Orson Welles—tantalizing, never-before-seen glimpses into the filmmaker’s rich inner life— Mark Cousins sheds new light on the experiences, dreams, desires, and obsessions that inspired his masterpieces.

title: The Fabelmans
synopsis: Growing up in post-World War II era Arizona, young Sammy Fabelman aspires to become a filmmaker as he reaches adolescence, but soon discovers a shattering family secret. He discovers how the power of films can help him see the truth.

title: The Face of the Jellyfish
synopsis: With no one able to provide an explanation, let alone offer a solution, one day Marina discovers that her face has changed completely. What do a face, a footprint or a name mean? What is it that makes us be who we are?

title: The Factory
synopsis: Visiting day. Lindalva prepares food to take to her son in jail. Metruti shaves and wears the best clothes to welcome his mother. Today is a very special day and he really needs to make a phone call. His mother is taking the risk to smuggle a cell phone for him into the penitentiary…

title: The Faithful Hussar
synopsis: The worst thing that can happen to a Rhinelander is a carnival ban. When his stern wife once again points her moral finger, her husband imagines a cunning plan which makes it possible for him and his friends to plunge into this “immoral swamp”.

title: The Fall
synopsis: In silent-era Hollywood, stuntman Roy Walker is brought to a hospital after an on-set accident. There, he befriends a young girl and tells her a fantastical tale of epic heroes taking revenge on an evil ruler. The story transports her from the hospital into the exotic landscapes of her imagination.

title: The Fall of Sir Douglas Weatherford
synopsis: Kenneth works as a tour guide dressing up as the town’s most notable historical figure, Sir Douglas Weatherford, who Kenneth proudly claims as his ancestor. When the village becomes the base of a big-budget fantasy TV show, his obsession with the show’s lead actor fuels a tragic downward spiral.

title: The Fall of the Louse of Usher
synopsis: After murdering his wife, a young man finds himself in a lunatic asylum. Once inside he’s at the mercy of Dr Calahari, whose alternative treatment gives rise to a series of weird and nightmarish hallucinations.

title: The Falling
synopsis: In an English girls’ school in 1969, sixteen-year-old Abbie and Lydia are best friends. A tragedy at the school triggers a mysterious epidemic among the pupils. The authorities claim there is nothing wrong, but Lydia refuses to accept this. In seeking the truth, she discovers a long-buried secret.

title: The Farewell
synopsis: Billi, a headstrong Chinese-American writer returns to China when her beloved grandmother is diagnosed with terminal cancer. She struggles with her family’s decision to keep Grandma in the dark about her own illness as they all stage an impromptu wedding to see Grandma one last time.

title: The Father
synopsis: Londoner Anthony is unwilling to admit to his daughter Anne that he is no longer able to care for himself. When Anne brings in a nurse, he goes from charming to insulting. One day Anthony wakes up bewildered in a home he doesn’t recognize, and it becomes clear how reality is slipping from his grasp.

title: The Father of My Children
synopsis: Grégoire Canvel has everything a man could want. A wife he loves, three delightful children and a stimulating job. He’s a film producer. Discovering talented filmmakers and developing films is precisely his reason for living. Yet his prestigious production company, Moon Films, is on its last legs.

title: The Favourite
synopsis: England, early 18th  century. England is at war with the French. A frail Queen Anne occupies the throne and her close friend Lady Sarah governs the country in her stead while tending to Anne’s ill health and mercurial temper. When a new servant Abigail arrives, her charm endears her to Sarah.

title: The Featherweight
synopsis: In 1964, a camera crew follows Willie Pep, retired featherweight boxing champion. Down and out in Hartford, Connecticut, married to a woman half his age, and dealing with a drug-addled son and mounting debts, Pep decides to make a return to the ring.

title: The Feeling That the Time for Doing Something Has Passed
synopsis: Ann, a morose New Yorker in her 30s, feels stuck in all areas of her life—the years have gone by quickly in her long-term casual BDSM relationship, low-level corporate job, and quarrelsome Jewish family. As she begins to feel increasingly alienated, she wrestles with herself and her relationships.

title: The Fiances
synopsis: Two Milanese fiancés’ strained relationship is tested when the man accepts a new job in Sicily. With the separation comes loneliness, nostalgia, and—perhaps—some new perspectives that might rejuvenate their love.

title: The Fire Within: A Requiem for Katia and Maurice Krafft
synopsis: Married volcanologists Katia and Maurice Krafft perished during the 1991 Mount Unzen eruption, leaving behind 200 hours of footage. This footage is transformed into a visually stunning tribute, letting volcanic imagery speak for itself.

title: Dead of Winter
synopsis: A woman, travelling alone through snowbound northern Minnesota, interrupts the kidnapping of a teenage girl. Hours from the nearest town and with no phone service, she realizes that she is the young girl’s only hope.

title: The Five Obstructions
synopsis: Lars von Trier challenges his mentor, Jørgen Leth, to remake his 1968 classic short film The Perfect Human five times, with a series of increasingly outlandish guidelines. Von Trier’s rules include, among others, that one film be shot in animation, and another in the most miserable place on earth.

title: The Flash
synopsis: Barry Allen travels back in time to prevent his mother’s murder, which brings unintentional consequences to his timeline.

title: The Flavor of Cassava Leaf Over Rice
synopsis: Married to a TV producer and living in the Hollywood Hills, novelist Michael Allegheny feels isolated and blocked. When his wife leaves town, he drifts into a medley of Chinese takeout, hot tub dips, and wine—until a chance meeting with an old flame rekindles memories and unresolved desires.

title: The Florida Project
synopsis: Set in the shadow of the most magical place on Earth, 6-year-old Moonee and her two best friends forge their own adventures. Meanwhile Moonee’s struggling mom and a kindhearted motel manager protect the kids from the harsh reality that surrounds them.

title: The Fog
synopsis: As a small California town nears its centenary, eerie events unfold: objects animate, Rev. Malone uncovers a town secret, announcer Stevie sees a mystic fire, and Elizabeth finds a mutilated fisherman. A luminous fog envelops the town, bringing death in its wake.

title: The Forest for the Trees
synopsis: Melanie leaves home to start her teaching career, armed with alternative methods and bright-eyed optimism. But after her first day of classes, during which she gives an ambitious speech, Melanie finds rejection from her colleagues, torture from her students, and loneliness in her personal life.

title: The Fortress
synopsis: Switzerland has some of the most restrictive asylum laws in all of Europe. In a Swiss reception center for asylum-seekers, 200 men, women, and children wait—some having risked their lives to be here—until the government decides their fate. The facility’s daily sorting process is examined close up.

title: The Foundry
synopsis: The men work in the foundry day and night. Finally, they get hungry. They eat lunch while watching the Lumière brothers’ film, Workers Leaving the Lumière Factory, in the foundry’s cinema.

title: The Fox Exploits the Tiger's Might
synopsis: A story about two teenagers who are having a struggle in their sexual awakening and the relations between power and sex, set in a military base ghost town.

title: The Friend
synopsis: Follows a story of love, friendship, grief and healing, about a writer who adopts a Great Dane that belonged to a late friend and mentor.

title: The Friends
synopsis: During their summer break, three curious schoolboys begin spying on a reclusive old man living in a secluded house. Soon, they discover an extraordinary man. As summer days go by, they develop a new outlook on life and death, deepening their appreciation for the value of their friendships.

title: The Funeral
synopsis: Cemal drives a hearse for a living. He’s entrusted with secretly carrying the body of a young girl at the request of her family. One night, he hears strange groans from the back of the truck, even though she the girl hasn’t got a pulse.

title: The Future
synopsis: Directionless thirty-somethings Sophie and Jason will soon adopt a cat that will need constant attention. With one month of freedom left, the couple decides to live life as if the world is ending, leading to quitting their jobs and the internet, creative confusion, adultery and stopping time itself.

title: The Future Tense
synopsis: When two Irish filmmakers travel home from London with their daughter, they reflect on their relationship to Ireland, and their adopted home, England. They explore their own experiences in ageing, parenting, and mental illness, along with the brutal history that lies beneath Ireland’s heavy earth.

title: The Garden of the Finzi-Continis
synopsis: The Finzi-Continis are among the most cultivated and old-established families in Ferrara, firmly rooted in tradition and part of the city’s Jewish intelligentsia. But, in the years leading up to World War II, the family is caught up in the politics of racial discrimination…

title: The Garden That Tilts
synopsis: In pursuit of their latest target, young hitmen Karl and Roland leave Paris for the country, where Kate—a woman some years their senior—resides. Endearing themselves to her companion Michel, they move into her lakeside mansion, where Karl, in spite of himself, becomes infatuated with Kate.

title: The General
synopsis: After being rejected by the Confederate military, not realizing it was due to his crucial civilian role, an engineer must single-handedly recapture his beloved locomotive after it is seized by Union spies and return it through enemy lines.

title: The Getting of Wisdom
synopsis: At a strict Melbourne finishing school in the late Victorian era, country girl Laura Tweedle Rambotham struggles against rigid expectations. Imaginative and defiant, she navigates friendship, rivalry, and heartbreak while refusing to conform to the model of a proper young lady.

title: The Girl and the Spider
synopsis: Lisa is moving out of her flatshare with Mara and Markus, and her move now means the end of an era. As boxes are shifted, walls painted and cupboards built, abysses begin to open up. While Lisa is looking forward to the change, for Mara it triggers a rollercoaster of emotions.

title: The Girl in the Arm-Chair
synopsis: Peggy is in love with Frank, but Frank does not return her affection. Frank falls in with a bad crowd. He loses heavily at cards and is forced to steal money from his father’s safe. Little did he know that Peggy was dozing in the armchair and awoke just in time to witness the theft.

title: The Girl with the Needle
synopsis: Struggling to survive in post-WWI Copenhagen, a newly unemployed and pregnant young woman is taken in by a charismatic elder to help run an underground adoption agency. The two form an unexpected bond, until a sudden discovery changes everything.

title: The Girls
synopsis: As they tour Sweden in a theatrical production of Lysistrata, performing to often uncomprehending audiences, three women find their own lives and marriages mirrored in Aristophanes’s play. Soon, onstage drama, offstage reality, and surrealist fantasies begin to collide.

title: The Maidens of Fetish Street
synopsis: One-of-a-kind “experimental grindhouse” film (and the sole effort by director Saul Resnick) is a series of kinky vignettes centered around a lonely, wandering soul, purportedly set in a 1928 Los Angeles that seems curiously infused with 1960s S&M iconography.

title: The Gleaners and I
synopsis: With a digital camcorder in hand, Agnès Varda sets out to make a documentary about the lives of gatherers, recyclers, and treasure hunters she encounters in the cities and countryside of France. Often sidetracked, Varda finds time to reflect on everything from aging to the birth of cinema.

title: The Gleaners and I: Two Years Later
synopsis: Agnes Varda returns to the people she met in her 2000 documentary on gleaning and meets some new people who were inspired by her first film.

title: The Goat
synopsis: After missing out on a fresh loaf of bread, an innocent and unsuspecting Buster winds up with his face mistakenly on a Wanted Dead or Alive poster all over town. On the run he ends up in the home of Virginia, only to come face to face with his worst fear.

title: The Goat Life
synopsis: The real-life incident of an Indian migrant worker, Najeeb Muhammad, who goes to Saudi Arabia to earn money. However, in a twist of fate, he finds himself living a slave-like existence, herding goats in the middle of the desert.

title: The Gold Bug
synopsis: Feminism, Victoria Benedictsson, Leandro N. Alem, the Radical Party in Argentina, suicide, stunts, Edgar Allan Poe, the complicated relationship between low-budget films with a political aim and the film industry.

title: The Gold Rush
synopsis: The Little Tramp heads to Alaska to try his luck at searching for gold. In this vast landscape packed with avalanches, the incomparable Tramp seeks his fortune, but faces more than he bargained for when he’s forced to deal with blizzards, criminals, and falling in love.

title: The Golden Coach
synopsis: Camilla, the star of a theater company, hesitates between three men. A young Spanish officer, the Viceroy who gives her his magnificent golden coach and Ramon, a torero, who offers her a share of his glory. As the threat of war looms, she soon finds she must make painful decisions about her future.

title: The Golden Glove
synopsis: Notorious German serial killer Fritz Honka haunted Hamburg’s red light district in the 1970s, frequenting his favorite bar, the “Golden Glove,” where drinking is a reflex against pain and longing. There, Honka, a pitiful loser chasing after lonely women, keeps more than just secrets in the attic.

title: The Good Boss
synopsis: Awaiting a visit by a committee that could give his company an award for excellence, the owner of an industrial scales manufacturing business tries to resolve any problems from his workers in enough time.

title: The Good Girls
synopsis: The year is 1982, and an economic crisis is hitting Mexico. The always charming, perfect, and spoiled Sofia, the queen bee of her friend group, faces the unimaginable—her social decay. Cracks appear in Sofia and her husband’s manicured lives as the social and economic order shifts around them.

title: The Good Sister
synopsis: Rose is very close to her older brother Sam. When a woman accuses him of rape, Rose is asked to testify in the investigation against him. This tests both their relationship and her moral integrity.

title: The Good Son
synopsis: At the age of 17, Iltima has spent much of his life looking after his mother, the mercurial Leila, a veteran actress who’s successful and famous enough to be constant tabloid fodder. His mother’s neglect has filled him with a rage he now considers normal.

title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
synopsis: Amid the chaos of the American Civil War, three gunslingers—Blondie, Tuco, and Angel Eyes—race to uncover a fortune in stolen Confederate gold. Each holds part of the secret to its location, but uneasy alliances and shifting loyalties lead them toward a legendary final showdown.

title: The Good, the Bad, the Weird
synopsis: In 1930s Manchuria, an encounter on a train triggers an epic crusade for a treasure map. Do-won, `the good’, is a bounty hunter, out to track down Chang-yee, `the bad’, a hitman attempting to pinch the map from a military official. However, the ruthless Tae-goo snaffles the map for himself.

title: The Gospel According to St. Matthew
synopsis: Starting from his Immaculate Conception, the life of Jesus is retraced according to the Gospel of St. Matthew. When Jesus begins to travel through Palestine with his disciples to spread the word of God, the Romans conspire to have him silenced, leading to his arrest, crucifixion, and resurrection.

title: The Graduate
synopsis: Recent college graduate Ben Braddock returns to his parents’ California home and stumbles through a haze of indecision as he navigates life in the real world. He starts an unexpected sexual relationship with the much older wife of his father’s business partner, only to fall for her daughter as well.

title: The Grand Siege
synopsis: Berna Tuna, a renowned academic has just died. Her husband Macit will take this opportunity to face past wrongdoings and reconnect with his long-neglected daughter İpek.

title: The Grandmaster
synopsis: In 1930s China, Ip Man is celebrated as the grandmaster of Wing Chun. During a competition, he wins the affection of Gong Er, a master of the northern Chinese martial arts tradition. But the occupation by Japanese troops separates the two, and they will only meet again after the war ends.

title: The Great Beauty
synopsis: Jep Gambardella, a 65-year-old journalist, lives among Rome’s decadent high society in a swirl of rooftop parties and late-night soirees. But when he learns of the death of his friend’s wife, a woman he once loved, his life is thrown into perspective and he begins to see the world through new eyes.

title: The Great Pretender
synopsis: In the off-off-Broadway world of New York theatre, lovesick French director Mona comes to America to mount a play based on her last relationship: a tumultuous affair with hard-living street photographer Nick. But soon, Mona develops feelings for the actor playing Nick.

title: Green Border
synopsis: In an attempt to provoke Europe, refugees from the Middle East and Africa are lured to the border by propaganda promising easy passage to the EU. Pawns in this hidden war, the lives of Julia, an activist who has given up her comfortable life, Jan, a border guard, and a Syrian family intertwine.

title: The Grief of Others
synopsis: The Ryries have suffered a loss: The death of a baby just after birth. Without words to express their grief, the parents try to return to their previous lives. The kids respond to the tensions around them in idiosyncratic ways. But as the family scatters into grief, an unexpected visitor arrives.

title: The Hand
synopsis: Hong Kong, 1960s. Zhang, a shy tailor’s assistant, is riveted by his imperious client Miss Hua. Upon meeting her, she seduces him to make sure he will truly remember her when designing her garments. After this intimate first encounter, a rapport develops between the two.

title: The Hand of God
synopsis: An awkward Italian teen struggling to find his place experiences heartbreak and liberation after he’s inadvertently saved from a freak accident by football legend Diego Maradona.

title: The Handmaiden
synopsis: In 1930s Korea, a new girl is hired as a handmaiden to a Japanese heiress who lives a secluded life on a large countryside estate. But the maid has a secret: She is a pickpocket recruited by a swindler, who is posing as a Japanese Count, to help him seduce the Lady and steal her fortune.

title: The Happiest Day in the Life of Olli Mäki
synopsis: Finland, 1962. Boxer Olli Mäki has a shot at the World Featherweight title. Immensely talented and equally modest, Olli’s small town life is transformed when he’s swept into national stardom and suddenly regarded as a symbol of his country. There’s only one problem: Olli has just fallen in love.

title: The Happy Version of Eden
synopsis: Santiago, 1973. In La Moneda presidential palace, President Salvador Allende wakes up to a call informing him that a military coup is taking place. His lover weeps. F-19 aircrafts poise to bomb La Moneda. Allende refuses to surrender. Bodies are thrown into the air.

title: The Harder They Come
synopsis: Ivan, a renegade rural musician, journeys to Kingston looking for fame and fortune, but only finds reality. Becoming an outlaw after a music producer refuses to play his record, Ivan achieves such notoriety while on the run from the police that eventually the producer can’t resist airing his music.

title: The Harder They Fall
synopsis: When outlaw Nat Love discovers that his enemy Rufus Buck is being released from prison he rounds up his gang to track Rufus down and seek revenge. Rufus Buck has his own fearsome crew, including “Treacherous” Trudy Smith and Cherokee Bill, and they are not a group that knows how to lose.

title: The Hateful Eight
synopsis: In snowy Wyoming, bounty hunter John Ruth is traveling to Red Rock to bring his fugitive captive Daisy Domergue to justice. On the way, fellow bounty hunter Major Warren joins them. Seeking refuge from a blizzard, they end up in a roadhouse, where they encounter a number of suspicious travelers.

title: The Headless Woman
synopsis: Veronica is driving in northwestern Argentina when her mobile phone distracts her and she runs over something—but drives on. The police confirm that there was no accident, but Veronica begins to have a meltdown, thinking she may have killed someone.  Was it an animal? A child? Or nothing at all?

title: The Heart of Amos Klein
synopsis: During a heart transplant operation, as he wavers between life and death, Amos Klein returns to the significant landmarks of his life. His journey is a reflection of the moral corruption within Israeli society, providing insight into the historic and cultural context from which it stems.

title: The Heart of the World
synopsis: It is both a spoof and a loving recreation of Soviet silent film. It is also an inventive movie in its own right. It is so full of images, one rapidly following the other in montage style, that it feels like it has the imagery and storytelling of a much longer movie.

title: The Heiresses
synopsis: Chela and Chiquita have been together for over 30 years. They both descend from wealthy families, but recently their financial situation has worsened and they have to sell off their inherited possessions. When Chiquita is imprisoned on fraud charges, Chela is forced to face a new reality.

title: The Herb of the Rat
synopsis: In a cemetery by the sea, two strangers meet. He takes care of her, creating a unique relationship by telling stories and photographing her. Until a mouse destroys the photos and he begins to realize that the mouse has a deeper role in his life than just destroying memories.

title: The Here After
synopsis: When teenager John returns to his rural hometown after serving time in prison, he finds that his crime is neither forgiven nor forgotten. The local community and students at his old high school struggle with his presence. Soon, John is being attacked by fellow pupils and strangers in shops.

title: The Hidden Tiger
synopsis: Juninho, Menor, Neguinho, Adilson and Eldo are young dwellers of Nacional’s neighborhood, Contagem’s suburbs. Divided between work and fun, crime and hope, each one of them will have to find ways to overcome the obstacles and tame the tiger they bring inside their veins.

title: The Holdovers
synopsis: A grumpy prep school instructor must stay on campus over Christmas break to supervise a few stranded students. Over time, he bonds with a rebellious, intelligent teen and the school’s grieving head cook, who recently lost her son in Vietnam.

title: The Holy Girl
synopsis: Amalia, an adolescent torn between her sexuality and Catholic faith, encounters Dr. Jano’s inappropriate advances. Repulsed yet inspired, she believes it’s her mission to save him. Becoming an unusual voyeur, she starts stalking the doctor.

title: The Honest One
synopsis: When an incorruptible government pay clerk, who isn’t taken seriously by anyone including his family, get robbed while carrying huge amount of work money, he becomes popular because people believe he faked the robbery and kept the money.

title: The Host
synopsis: When a young girl is snatched away from her father by an unidentified giant creature that emerges from the Han River to wreak havoc on Seoul, her entire family sets out to locate the horrifying monster and bring their little girl back home to safety.

title: The House by the Cemetery
synopsis: The Boyles buy a house in rural New England. Little do they know, the previous owner was the deranged Dr. Freudstein, whose experiments left behind a legacy of bloody mayhem. Now the devilish doctor sets his sights on the young family, and home sweet home is about to become a horrific hell on earth.

title: The House That Jack Built
synopsis: Failed architect Jack recounts his elaborately orchestrated murders. Told from his point of view, he sees each one as a towering work of art that defines his life’s work as a serial killer. As the police is drawing nearer, Jack takes greater risks in his attempt to create the ultimate artwork.

title: The Housemaid
synopsis: Eun-yi is hired as a maid in a mansion owned by a wealthy businessman. He quickly starts seducing his employee who seemingly has little choice but to comply with his sexual advances. Soon the women of the family plot against Eun-yi who must fight an equally devious battle to protect herself.

title: The Human Surge
synopsis: Buenos Aires. Exe, 25 years old, has just lost his job and is not looking for another one. Online, he meets Alf, a boy from Mozambique who is also bored with his job and who is about to follow Archie, another boy who has run away into the jungle.

title: The Human Voice
synopsis: A woman watches time passing next to the suitcases of her ex-lover (who is supposed to come pick them up, but never arrives) and a restless dog who doesn’t understand that his master has abandoned him. Two living beings facing abandonment.

title: The Humans
synopsis: Over the course of one night, the Blake family comes together for Thanksgiving at Brigid and her partner Richard’s lower Manhattan apartment. As the evening unfolds, the family member’s frustrations and insecurities are exposed as home truths and long-hidden secrets rise to the surface.

title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes
synopsis: The film centers on Coriolanus Snow well before he takes power as the tyrannical president. At 18 years old, he’s looking to restore honor to his lineage which has fallen on hard times in the aftermath of a war in the Capitol.

title: The Hunt
synopsis: A middle-aged kindergarten teacher is slowly piecing his life back together following a divorce and custody battle. He has a new love, a new job and is patching things up with his estranged son. But a little white lie from one of his students is about to shatter his newfound luck.

title: The Hurt Locker
synopsis: When a new sergeant, James, takes over a highly trained bomb disposal team amidst the Iraq War, he surprises his two subordinates, Sanborn and Eldridge, by recklessly plunging them into a deadly game of urban combat. As the men struggle to control their new leader, the city explodes into chaos.

title: The Hypnosis
synopsis: Vera and André are a couple who get the chance to pitch their startup business at a prestigious pitching competition. Right before the contest, Vera tries hypnotherapy to quit smoking, with an unexpected side effect: she loses all social inhibitions.

title: The Idiots
synopsis: In the Copenhagen suburbs, a commune aims to disrupt bourgeois society by spontaneously feigning physical or mental disabilities in public—including a new recruit motivated by a secret sorrow.

title: The Idol
synopsis: A film about the popular Palestinian singer Mohammad Assaf, from childhood to adulthood, and his life in Gaza until he won the Arab idol.

title: The Image Book
synopsis: Godard’s film essay delves into cinema’s history and modern world. He manipulates film fragments, reflecting on dissonance. His voice guides through metaphysical musings, anchored by an obsession with images, offering a poignant ontological exploration.

title: The Innkeepers
synopsis: During the final days at the Yankee Pedlar Inn, two employees determined to reveal the hotel’s haunted past begin to experience disturbing events as old guests check in for a stay.

title: The Innocent
synopsis: Abel, a museum educator and widower, is at his wits’ end when his mother Sylvie rushes into a marriage with Michel, a convict she met while teaching drama at the local prison. Abel disapproves, and he decides to stalk his new stepfather, bringing along his best friend Clémence for the ride.

title: The Innocents
synopsis: During the bright Nordic summer, a group of children reveal their dark and mysterious powers when the adults aren’t looking. But what starts out as innocent playtime soon turns dark and violent.

title: The Inspection
synopsis: A young, gay Black man, rejected by his mother and with few options for his future, decides to join the Marines, doing whatever it takes to succeed in a system that would cast him aside.

title: The Invisible Life of Euridice Gusmão
synopsis: Rio de Janeiro, 1950. Euridice and Guida are two inseparable sisters. Each has a dream: becoming a renowned pianist, or finding true love. Because of their father they are forced to live without one another. Separated, they take control of their destiny, while never giving up hope of being reunited.

title: The Invisible Man
synopsis: When Cecilia’s abusive ex takes his own life and leaves her his fortune, she suspects his death was a hoax. As a series of coincidences turn lethal, Cecilia works to prove that she is being hunted by someone nobody can see.

title: The Invitation
synopsis: Will and Eden were once a loving couple, then they separated after a tragedy took their son. Two years later, Eden returns with a new husband and invites Will to a dinner party, which quickly becomes an unsettling affair that reopens old wounds and creates new tensions.

title: The Irishman
synopsis: An epic saga of organized crime told through the eyes of WWII veteran Frank Sheeran—a hustler and hitman who worked with some of the most notorious figures of the 20th century— chronicling one of the greatest unsolved mysteries in U.S. history, the disappearance of legendary union boss Jimmy Hoffa.

title: The Iron Claw
synopsis: The inseparable Von Erich brothers made history in the intensely competitive world of professional wrestling in the early 1980s. Through tragedy and triumph, under the shadow of their domineering father and coach, the brothers seek larger-than-life immortality on the biggest stage in sports.

title: The Jester
synopsis: Over a sleepless night in 1978, Francisco and Rita meet up to remember João, a recently murdered childhood friend. João had belonged to a subversive leftist group during the dictatorship, and was a film producer who had been filming an adaptation of Alexandre Herculano’s drama The Jester.

title: The Journey
synopsis: During the 2006 Northern Ireland peace talks, Sinn Féin leader Martin McGuinness and Democratic Unionist Party leader Ian Paisley are forced to travel by car together. Over the detour-filled trip through the Scottish countryside, they will take the biggest leap of faith and change.

title: The Juniper Tree
synopsis: Set in medieval Iceland, Margit and her older sister Katla flee for safety after their mother is burned to death for witchcraft. Finding shelter  with Johan and his resentful young son, Jonas, the sisters help form an impromptu family unit that’s soon strained by Katla’s burgeoning sorcery.

title: The Killer
synopsis: Chow Yun-fat stars as a killer with a conscience in John Woo’s exquisite dissection of morals in a corrupt society. Replete with balletic, slow-motion gun battles on the streets of Hong Kong, The Killer mixes genres from both the East and the West.

title: The Killer
synopsis: An assassin begins to psychologically crack as he develops a conscience, even as his clients continue to demand his skills.
